NAME

video - Video

SYNOPSIS

Scalar ASCII background color rendering. file ansi.c

Luminance Calculation Constants


#define LUMA_RED 77
Luminance red coefficient (0.299 * 256 = 77). #define LUMA_GREEN 150
Luminance green coefficient (0.587 * 256 = 150). #define LUMA_BLUE 29
Luminance blue coefficient (0.114 * 256 = 29). #define LUMA_THRESHOLD 128
Luminance threshold for rounding.

Detailed Description

Implements a Matrix-inspired digital rain effect that can be applied to ASCII frames as a post-processing step. The effect creates the illusion of "falling" characters by modulating brightness in a sawtooth wave pattern.

ALGORITHM OVERVIEW:

The digital rain effect is based on the observation that Matrix code rain isn't actually falling characters - it's stationary characters whose brightness changes to create the illusion of rain. Key concepts:

1.
Sawtooth Wave: Each column has a brightness wave that repeats, creating multiple "raindrops" falling at different speeds.
2.
Cursor/Tracer: The bright leading character is detected where brightness increases (where the wave rises).
3.
Organic Variation: Random timing offsets and wobble functions prevent mechanical-looking patterns.

IMPLEMENTATION:

  • Per-column state tracking (timing, speed, phase)
  • Brightness calculation using fractional time wrapping
  • ANSI color code injection for brightness modulation
  • Cursor highlighting with increased brightness
  • Smooth brightness transitions via decay blending

Note

This effect is applied AFTER ASCII conversion

Works with both color and monochrome ASCII frames

Minimal performance impact (O(frame_size) single pass)

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

February 2026

This header provides comprehensive functions for converting images to ASCII art and outputting frames to the terminal. Supports multiple color modes, aspect ratio preservation, capability-aware conversion, and frame layout management.

CORE FEATURES:

  • Image-to-ASCII conversion with color support
  • Terminal capability-aware conversion (auto-selects optimal color mode)
  • Aspect ratio preservation for proper image proportions
  • Terminal frame output and management
  • Frame padding and layout utilities (horizontal and vertical)
  • Grid layout for multiple frames (multi-user support)
  • Buffer pool integration for efficient memory management
  • SIMD-optimized conversion for performance

CONVERSION MODES:

The system supports multiple conversion modes:

  • Standard conversion: Basic image-to-ASCII with optional color
  • Capability-aware: Automatically selects best color mode for terminal
  • Aspect ratio preservation: Maintains image proportions (terminal correction)
  • Stretch mode: Fills terminal dimensions exactly (may distort proportions)

FRAME MANAGEMENT:

Frame management includes:

  • Terminal initialization and cleanup
  • Frame output to terminal
  • Frame padding (horizontal and vertical)
  • Grid layout for multiple frames
  • Frame source management

Note

All conversion functions return allocated strings that must be freed.

Terminal initialization functions must be matched with cleanup.

Capability-aware conversion is recommended for optimal results.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

September 2025

This header provides AVX2 (Advanced Vector Extensions 2) optimized functions for converting images to ASCII art on modern x86-64 CPUs.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

September 2025

This header provides SIMD-optimized functions for converting images to ASCII art. Supports multiple architectures including SSE2, SSSE3, AVX2 (x86), NEON (ARM), and SVE (ARM).

The interface provides:

  • SIMD initialization and setup
  • Architecture-specific rendering functions
  • Benchmarking utilities
  • Cache management
  • UTF-8 character handling

Note

SIMD support is automatically detected and enabled based on CPU capabilities. CMake controls SIMD levels via ENABLE_SIMD_* options.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

October 2025

This header provides NEON (Advanced SIMD) optimized functions for converting images to ASCII art on ARM processors.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

August 2025

This header provides a dynamic output buffer system with automatic expansion and optimized ANSI escape sequence emission functions. The buffer is designed for efficient ASCII art frame construction and terminal output.

CORE FEATURES:

  • Auto-expanding output buffer (grows as needed)
  • Efficient buffer management operations
  • Optimized ANSI escape sequence emission
  • Run-length encoding (RLE) support for compression
  • Color mode auto-selection (16-color, 256-color, truecolor)
  • Integer-to-string conversion utilities

BUFFER MANAGEMENT:

The output buffer automatically expands when space is needed:

  • Initial capacity is allocated on first reservation
  • Buffer doubles in size when capacity is exceeded
  • Memory is allocated on-demand (no upfront allocation)
  • Buffer ownership: caller must free buffer when done

ANSI ESCAPE SEQUENCES:

The system emits ANSI escape sequences for:

  • Color output (foreground and background)
  • Cursor control and positioning
  • Reset and formatting codes
  • Run-length encoding for repeated characters

COLOR MODE AUTO-SELECTION:

Color emission functions automatically select optimal mode:

  • 16-color: Uses standard ANSI color codes (0-15)
  • 256-color: Uses extended palette (16-255)
  • Truecolor: Uses 24-bit RGB escape sequences

Note

All buffer operations ensure null-terminated strings.

Buffer memory must be freed by caller using free() on buf pointer.

ANSI sequences are optimized for minimal terminal I/O.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

September 2025

This module provides functions for working with ANSI RLE (Run-Length Encoding) escape sequences. The REP sequence (CSI Ps b) repeats the preceding graphic character Ps times.

RLE FORMAT:

The ANSI REP sequence format is: ESC [ N b

  • ESC (0x1b) - Escape character
  • [ - CSI introducer
  • N - Decimal repeat count (how many times to repeat)
  • b - REP command character

Example: "A\x1b[3b" means "A" followed by 3 more "A"s = "AAAA"

USE CASES:

  • Compression: Reduce bandwidth for ASCII art with repeated characters
  • Expansion: Convert RLE to plain text for file output or non-RLE terminals

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

December 2025

This header provides scalar (non-SIMD) halfblock rendering functions for converting RGB images to ANSI-escaped halfblock characters with truecolor foreground and background colors.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

February 2026

This header provides SSE2 (Streaming SIMD Extensions 2) optimized functions for converting images to ASCII art on x86-64 CPUs.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

August 2025

This header provides SSSE3 (Supplemental Streaming SIMD Extensions 3) optimized functions for converting images to ASCII art on x86-64 CPUs.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

August 2025

This header provides SVE (Scalable Vector Extension) optimized functions for converting images to ASCII art on ARM processors with SVE support.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

August 2025

This module provides color filtering capabilities to apply monochromatic color tints to grayscale video feeds. The server processes color filters; clients see each user in their chosen filter color.

FEATURES:

  • 11 built-in color filters (matrix green, cyan, magenta, etc.)
  • ITU-R BT.601 grayscale conversion formula
  • Two colorization modes: "color on white" and "white on color"
  • Per-pixel colorization for maximum quality
  • Protocol integration via CLIENT_CAPABILITIES packet

COLOR FILTERS:

0.
none (no filtering)
1.
black (dark content on white background)
2.
white (white content on black background)
3.
green (#00FF41)
4.
magenta (#FF00FF)
5.
fuchsia (#FF00AA)
6.
orange (#FF8800)
7.
teal (#00DDDD)
8.
cyan (#00FFFF)
9.
pastel-pink (#FFB6C1)
10.
error-red (#FF3333)
11.
yellow (#FFEB99)

ARCHITECTURE:

  • color_filter_registry[]: Metadata for all 11 filters (name, CLI name, RGB values, mode)
  • rgb_to_grayscale(): ITU-R BT.601 conversion (efficient fixed-point math)
  • colorize_grayscale_pixel(): Single pixel colorization (inline for performance)
  • apply_color_filter(): Image-wide colorization (in-place operation)

USAGE: 

// Convert image to grayscale and apply filter
if (filter != COLOR_FILTER_NONE) {


    apply_color_filter(image, width, height, filter);
}

PROTOCOL INTEGRATION:

The color_filter field is included in the CLIENT_CAPABILITIES packet:

  • Client sends chosen filter to server during connection
  • Server applies filter to client's video before ASCII conversion
  • All clients see the filtered output

Note

All filter colors are applied using ITU-R BT.601 grayscale conversion

Filters automatically set --color-mode mono (monochromatic display)

"--color-filter foo --color-mode 256" is an error

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

February 2026

This header provides comprehensive image data structures and operations for ascii-chat. Supports RGB images with standard and SIMD-optimized formats for efficient image processing and ASCII art conversion.

CORE FEATURES:

  • RGB image data structures (packed and SIMD-aligned)
  • Image allocation and memory management
  • Buffer pool integration for video pipeline efficiency
  • Image-to-ASCII conversion with multiple color modes
  • Color quantization and palette generation
  • Image resizing (nearest-neighbor and bilinear interpolation)
  • ANSI color code generation and conversion
  • Dithering support for improved color accuracy

IMAGE FORMATS:

The system supports:

  • Standard RGB24 format (packed, 3 bytes per pixel)
  • SIMD-aligned RGB format (4-byte aligned for NEON/AVX optimization)
  • Maximum 4K resolution (3840x2160)
  • Memory-efficient buffer pool allocation

COLOR MODES:

ASCII conversion supports multiple color modes:

  • Monochrome: No color, character-based only
  • 16-color: Standard ANSI colors
  • 256-color: Extended ANSI palette
  • Truecolor: 24-bit RGB (terminal-dependent)
  • Dithering: Floyd-Steinberg dithering for color accuracy

Note

All image allocation functions must be matched with corresponding destroy functions (image_destroy() or image_destroy_to_pool()).

ASCII conversion functions return allocated strings that must be freed by the caller.

Image structures are compatible with webcam capture (image_t).

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

September 2025

Macro Definition Documentation

#define ANSI_BG_PREFIX ' 33[48;2;'

#include <include/ascii-chat/video/ascii/ascii.h>

ANSI background color prefix (truecolor mode). ANSI escape sequence prefix for 24-bit truecolor background colors. Format: ESC[48;2;r;g;bm (where r, g, b are RGB values).

Note

Use with ANSI_COLOR_SUFFIX to form complete escape sequence.

Example: ANSI_BG_PREFIX "255;0;0" ANSI_COLOR_SUFFIX (red background).

Definition at line 487 of file ascii.h.

#define ANSI_COLOR_SUFFIX 'm'

#include <include/ascii-chat/video/ascii/ascii.h>

ANSI color suffix. ANSI escape sequence suffix for color escape sequences. Used with ANSI_FG_PREFIX or ANSI_BG_PREFIX to form complete escape sequences.

Note

Format: prefix + RGB + suffix = complete ANSI sequence.

Example: "\033[38;2;255;0;0m" (red foreground).

Definition at line 501 of file ascii.h.

#define ANSI_FG_PREFIX ' 33[38;2;'

#include <include/ascii-chat/video/ascii/ascii.h>

ANSI foreground color prefix (truecolor mode). ANSI escape sequence prefix for 24-bit truecolor foreground colors. Format: ESC[38;2;r;g;bm (where r, g, b are RGB values).

Note

Use with ANSI_COLOR_SUFFIX to form complete escape sequence.

Example: ANSI_FG_PREFIX "255;0;0" ANSI_COLOR_SUFFIX (red foreground).

#define
  ASCII_LUMINANCE_LEVELS 256


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/ascii/ascii.h>


Number of luminance levels supported (256). Number of luminance
    levels used for character mapping. Each pixel's brightness value (0-255)
    maps to a character in the luminance palette.




Note


256 levels correspond to 8-bit pixel brightness values.



Luminance palette must have 256 elements.

#define
  ASCII_SLEEP_NS 50000L


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/ascii/ascii.h>


Sleep duration in nanoseconds between frames (50000 ns = 50
    μs). Sleep duration between frame outputs for frame rate limiting.
    Used to prevent excessive terminal I/O and control frame rate.




Note


50000 nanoseconds = 50 microseconds = 0.05 milliseconds



Used for frame rate limiting in video playback.

#define
  ascii_zzz()


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/ascii/ascii.h>Value:


time_sleep_ns(ASCII_SLEEP_NS)






Sleep for frame rate limiting. Sleeps for a short duration (50
    microseconds) to limit frame rate and prevent excessive terminal I/O. Uses
    time_sleep_ns() for precise nanosecond-precision timing control with
    the unified timing API.




Note


Sleep duration: 50000 nanoseconds (50 microseconds)



Used between frame outputs for rate limiting.

char
  * ansi_compress_rle (const char * input, size_t input_len)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/ascii/rle.h>


Compress repeated characters using RLE escape sequences.


Parameters


input Input string (may contain ANSI escape codes)


input_len Length of input string


Returns


Newly allocated string with RLE compression (caller must
  free)


Compresses runs of repeated printable characters using ANSI RLE
    sequences. Only compresses when profitable (run length > RLE overhead).
    Preserves all existing escape sequences.




Note


The returned string must be freed by the caller using
  free().



Returns NULL if input is NULL or allocation fails.




Only compresses runs where RLE saves bytes (typically 4+
  chars).




Example








const char *frame = "AAAAAAA\x1b[31mBBBBB\n";
char *compressed = ansi_compress_rle(frame, strlen(frame));
// compressed = "A\x1b[6b\x1b[31mB\x1b[4b\n"
free(compressed);





Definition at line 98 of file rle.c.


98                                                              {
99   if (!input || input_len == 0) {
100     return NULL;
101   }
102 
103   outbuf_t ob = {0};
104   ob_reserve(&ob, input_len);
105 
106   size_t i = 0;
107   while (i < input_len) {
108     // Check for ESC character (start of ANSI sequence)
109     if (input[i] == '\x1b' && i + 1 < input_len && input[i + 1] == '[') {
110       size_t seq_start = i;
111       i += 2; // Skip ESC[
112 
113       // Skip parameter bytes
114       while (i < input_len && ((input[i] >= '0' && input[i] <= '9') || input[i] == ';')) {
115         i++;
116       }
117 
118       // Skip final byte
119       if (i < input_len) {
120         i++;
121       }
122 
123       // Copy entire escape sequence as-is
124       ob_write(&ob, input + seq_start, i - seq_start);
125     } else {
126       // Regular character - check for runs
127       char c = input[i];
128 
129       // Only compress printable characters (not newlines, not control chars)
130       if (c >= 0x20 && c != 0x7F) {
131         // Count run length
132         size_t run_len = 1;
133         i++;
134 
135         while (i < input_len && input[i] == c) {
136           run_len++;
137           i++;
138         }
139 
140         // Emit first character
141         ob_putc(&ob, c);
142 
143         // Use RLE if profitable (run > overhead of ESC[Nb)
144         if (run_len > 1 && rep_is_profitable((uint32_t)run_len)) {
145           emit_rep(&ob, (uint32_t)(run_len - 1));
146         } else {
147           // Emit remaining characters directly
148           for (size_t k = 1; k < run_len; k++) {
149             ob_putc(&ob, c);
150           }
151         }
152       } else {
153         // Non-compressible character (newline, etc.)
154         ob_putc(&ob, c);
155         i++;
156       }
157     }
158   }
159 
160   ob_term(&ob);
161   return ob.buf;
162 }






References outbuf_t::buf, emit_rep(),
    ob_putc(), ob_reserve(), ob_term(), ob_write(),
    and rep_is_profitable().






char
  * ansi_expand_rle (const char * input, size_t input_len)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/ascii/rle.h>


Expand RLE escape sequences in a string.


Parameters


input Input string containing ANSI escape codes


input_len Length of input string


Returns


Newly allocated string with RLE expanded (caller must
  free)


Expands ANSI RLE (REP) sequences while preserving all other escape
    codes:


    
  
  • RLE sequences (ESC[Nb) are expanded: previous character repeated N
    times
    • 
  
  • All other escape sequences (colors, cursor, etc.) are preserved as-is
    • 



This is useful when outputting to files/pipes where terminals
    can't interpret RLE, but you still want to preserve color codes.




Note


The returned string must be freed by the caller using
  free().



Returns NULL if input is NULL or allocation fails.




Example








const char *frame = "A\x1b[3b\x1b[31mB\n";  // A + repeat 3 + red + B
char *expanded = ansi_expand_rle(frame, strlen(frame));
// expanded = "AAAA\x1b[31mB\n" (RLE expanded, color preserved)
free(expanded);





Definition at line 13 of file rle.c.


13                                                            {
14   if (!input || input_len == 0) {
15     return NULL;
16   }
17 
18   // RLE expansion can make output larger than input, start with 2x capacity
19   outbuf_t ob = {0};
20   ob_reserve(&ob, input_len * 2);
21 
22   // Track the last character/grapheme for RLE expansion
23   // UTF-8 characters can be up to 4 bytes
24   char last_char[5] = " ";
25   size_t last_char_len = 1;
26 
27   size_t i = 0;
28   while (i < input_len) {
29     // Check for ESC character (start of ANSI sequence)
30     if (input[i] == '\x1b' && i + 1 < input_len && input[i + 1] == '[') {
31       size_t seq_start = i;
32       i += 2; // Skip ESC[
33 
34       // Parse parameter bytes (digits and semicolons)
35       uint32_t param = 0;
36       while (i < input_len && ((input[i] >= '0' && input[i] <= '9') || input[i] == ';')) {
37         if (input[i] >= '0' && input[i] <= '9') {
38           param = param * 10 + (uint32_t)(input[i] - '0');
39         } else if (input[i] == ';') {
40           param = 0; // Reset for next parameter
41         }
42         i++;
43       }
44 
45       // Check for final byte
46       if (i < input_len) {
47         char final_byte = input[i];
48         i++;
49 
50         // Handle RLE: ESC[Nb repeats previous character N times
51         if (final_byte == 'b' && param > 0) {
52           for (uint32_t r = 0; r < param; r++) {
53             ob_write(&ob, last_char, last_char_len);
54           }
55         } else {
56           // Not RLE - copy the entire escape sequence as-is
57           ob_write(&ob, input + seq_start, i - seq_start);
58         }
59       }
60     } else {
61       // Regular character - copy to output and track for RLE
62       unsigned char c = (unsigned char)input[i];
63       size_t char_len = 1;
64 
65       // Determine UTF-8 character length from first byte
66       if ((c & 0x80) == 0) {
67         char_len = 1; // ASCII
68       } else if ((c & 0xE0) == 0xC0) {
69         char_len = 2; // 2-byte UTF-8
70       } else if ((c & 0xF0) == 0xE0) {
71         char_len = 3; // 3-byte UTF-8
72       } else if ((c & 0xF8) == 0xF0) {
73         char_len = 4; // 4-byte UTF-8
74       }
75 
76       // Make sure we don't read past end of input
77       if (i + char_len > input_len) {
78         char_len = input_len - i;
79       }
80 
81       // Copy full UTF-8 character to output
82       ob_write(&ob, input + i, char_len);
83 
84       // Track last printable character for RLE (skip control chars)
85       if (c >= 0x20 && c != 0x7F) {
86         SAFE_MEMCPY(last_char, sizeof(last_char), input + i, char_len);
87         last_char[char_len] = '\0';
88         last_char_len = char_len;
89       }
90       i += char_len;
91     }
92   }
93 
94   ob_term(&ob);
95   return ob.buf;
96 }






References outbuf_t::buf, ob_reserve(),
    ob_term(), ob_write(), and SAFE_MEMCPY.






int
  apply_color_filter (uint8_t * pixels, uint32_t width,
  uint32_t height, uint32_t stride, color_filter_t filter,
  float time)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/color_filter.h>


Apply color filter to entire image in-place.


Parameters


pixels Image pixel buffer (modified in-place)


width Image width in pixels


height Image height in pixels


stride Bytes per row (typically width * 3 for RGB24)


filter Color filter to apply (COLOR_FILTER_NONE = no-op)


time Current time in seconds (used for COLOR_FILTER_RAINBOW
  animation)


Returns


0 on success, -1 on error (invalid parameters)


Applies the specified color filter to an RGB image. The
  operation:



  
1.

  
Converts each RGB pixel to grayscale (ITU-R BT.601)

  
2.

  
Scales grayscale by the filter's RGB color values

  
3.

  
Modifies pixels in-place




For "color on white" mode (black-on-white only): Dark
    pixels get full color, light pixels become white




For "white on color" mode (all other filters): Grayscale
    value scales the color intensity




For COLOR_FILTER_RAINBOW: Uses time parameter to cycle through RGB
    spectrum (3.5s period)




Note


This function modifies the image in-place



If filter == COLOR_FILTER_NONE, this is a no-op




Expected stride is typically width * 3 for RGB24 format




Definition at line 274 of file color_filter.c.


275                                    {
276   if (!pixels || width == 0 || height == 0 || stride == 0) {
277     return -1;
278   }
279 
280   if (filter == COLOR_FILTER_NONE) {
281     return 0; // No-op for none filter
282   }
283 
284   // Special handling for rainbow filter
285   if (filter == COLOR_FILTER_RAINBOW) {
286     uint8_t rainbow_r, rainbow_g, rainbow_b;
287     color_filter_calculate_rainbow(time, &rainbow_r, &rainbow_g, &rainbow_b);
288 
289     // Create temporary filter def with current rainbow color
290     color_filter_def_t rainbow_filter = {
291         .name = "rainbow",
292         .cli_name = "rainbow",
293         .r = rainbow_r,
294         .g = rainbow_g,
295         .b = rainbow_b,
296         .foreground_on_bg = false,
297     };
298 
299     // Process each pixel with minimum brightness to prevent black
300     // Minimum brightness is 70% to ensure rainbow colors stay vibrant even for dark input
301     const uint8_t min_brightness = 179; // 70% of 255
302 
303     for (uint32_t y = 0; y < height; y++) {
304       uint8_t *row = pixels + y * stride;
305       for (uint32_t x = 0; x < width; x++) {
306         uint8_t *pixel = row + x * 3;
307         uint8_t r = pixel[0];
308         uint8_t g = pixel[1];
309         uint8_t b = pixel[2];
310         uint8_t gray = rgb_to_grayscale(r, g, b);
311 
312         // Boost grayscale to maintain minimum brightness for rainbow
313         // Formula: adjusted = min + gray * (1 - min/255)
314         // This ensures: black pixels -> min_brightness, white pixels -> 255
315         uint8_t adjusted_gray = min_brightness + (uint8_t)((gray * (255U - min_brightness)) / 255U);
316 
317         colorize_grayscale_pixel(adjusted_gray, &rainbow_filter, &pixel[0], &pixel[1], &pixel[2]);
318       }
319     }
320     return 0;
321   }
322 
323   const color_filter_def_t *filter_def = color_filter_get_metadata(filter);
324   if (!filter_def) {
325     return -1; // Invalid filter
326   }
327 
328   // Process each pixel in the image
329   for (uint32_t y = 0; y < height; y++) {
330     uint8_t *row = pixels + y * stride;
331     for (uint32_t x = 0; x < width; x++) {
332       uint8_t *pixel = row + x * 3; // Assume RGB24 (3 bytes per pixel)
333       uint8_t r = pixel[0];
334       uint8_t g = pixel[1];
335       uint8_t b = pixel[2];
336 
337       // Convert RGB to grayscale using ITU-R BT.601
338       uint8_t gray = rgb_to_grayscale(r, g, b);
339 
340       // Colorize the grayscale value
341       colorize_grayscale_pixel(gray, filter_def, &pixel[0], &pixel[1], &pixel[2]);
342     }
343   }
344 
345   return 0;
346 }

    color_filter_get_metadata(), COLOR_FILTER_NONE, and
    COLOR_FILTER_RAINBOW.

    session_display_convert_to_ascii().






char
  * ascii_convert (image_t * original, const ssize_t width, const ssize_t
  height, const bool color, const bool aspect_ratio, const
  bool stretch, const char * palette_chars, const char
  luminance_palette[256])


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/ascii/ascii.h>


Convert image to ASCII art.


Parameters


original Source image (must not be NULL)


width Target width in characters (must be > 0)


height Target height in characters (must be > 0)


color Enable color output (true for color, false for monochrome)


aspect_ratio Preserve aspect ratio (true to maintain
  proportions, false to fill dimensions)


stretch Stretch to fit (if aspect_ratio is false, true to fill exactly,
  false to fit within)


palette_chars Character palette to use (or NULL for default ASCII
  palette)


luminance_palette Luminance-to-character mapping palette (must not be
  NULL, 256 elements)


Returns


Allocated ASCII frame string (caller must free), or NULL
  on error


Converts an image to ASCII art with specified dimensions and color
    mode. Uses character palette to map pixel luminance to ASCII characters and
    optionally applies color output using ANSI escape sequences.




CONVERSION PROCESS:


    
  
  • Resizes image to target dimensions (with aspect ratio preservation if
      enabled)
    • 
  
  • Maps each pixel's luminance to ASCII character using palette
    • 
  
  • Optionally applies color output using ANSI escape sequences
    • 
  
  • Returns null-terminated string containing ASCII frame
    • 



Note


Returns NULL on error (invalid image, memory allocation
  failure).



ASCII string must be freed by caller using free().




Aspect ratio preservation uses terminal character correction.




Color output requires terminal color support for proper
  display.




Definition at line 70 of file ascii.c.


72                                                        {
73   if (original == NULL || !palette_chars || !luminance_palette) {
74     log_error("ascii_convert: invalid parameters");
75     return NULL;
76   }
77 
78   // Check for empty strings
79   if (palette_chars[0] == '\0' || luminance_palette[0] == '\0') {
80     log_error("ascii_convert: empty palette strings");
81     return NULL;
82   }
83 
84   // Start with the target dimensions requested by the user (or detected from
85   // the terminal). These can be modified by aspect_ratio() if stretching is
86   // disabled and one of the dimensions was left to be calculated
87   // automatically.
88   ssize_t resized_width = width;
89   ssize_t resized_height = height;
90 
91   // If stretch is enabled, use full dimensions, otherwise calculate aspect ratio
92   if (_aspect_ratio) {
93     // The server now provides images at width*2 x height pixels
94     // The aspect_ratio function will handle terminal character aspect ratio
95     aspect_ratio(original->w, original->h, resized_width, resized_height, stretch, &resized_width, &resized_height);
96   }
97 
98   // Calculate padding for centering
99   size_t pad_width = 0;
100   size_t pad_height = 0;
101 
102   if (_aspect_ratio) {
103     // Only calculate padding when not stretching
104     ssize_t pad_width_ss = width > resized_width ? (width - resized_width) / 2 : 0;
105     pad_width = (size_t)pad_width_ss;
106 
107     ssize_t pad_height_ss = height > resized_height ? (height - resized_height) / 2 : 0;
108     pad_height = (size_t)pad_height_ss;
109   }
110 
111   // Resize the captured frame to the aspect-correct dimensions.
112   if (resized_width <= 0 || resized_height <= 0) {
113     log_error("Invalid dimensions for resize: width=%zd, height=%zd", resized_width, resized_height);
114     return NULL;
115   }
116 
117   // Validate dimensions fit in image_t's int fields before casting
118   if (resized_width > INT_MAX || resized_height > INT_MAX) {
119     log_error("Dimensions exceed INT_MAX: width=%zd, height=%zd", resized_width, resized_height);
120     return NULL;
121   }
122 
123   // Always resize to target dimensions
124   image_t *resized = image_new((size_t)resized_width, (size_t)resized_height);
125   if (!resized) {
126     log_error("Failed to allocate resized image");
127     return NULL;
128   }
129 
130   image_clear(resized);
131   image_resize(original, resized);
132 
133   char *ascii;
134   if (color) {
135     // Check for half-block mode first
136     if (GET_OPTION(render_mode) == RENDER_MODE_HALF_BLOCK) {
137       const uint8_t *rgb_data = (const uint8_t *)resized->pixels;
138 #if SIMD_SUPPORT_NEON
139       // Use NEON half-block renderer (optimized SIMD path)
140       log_dev("Using NEON halfblock renderer");
141       ascii = rgb_to_truecolor_halfblocks_neon(rgb_data, resized->w, resized->h, 0);
142 #else
143       // Fallback to scalar halfblock renderer (works on all platforms)
144       log_dev("Using scalar halfblock renderer (NEON not available)");
145       ascii = rgb_to_truecolor_halfblocks_scalar(rgb_data, resized->w, resized->h, 0);
146 #endif
147     } else {
148 #ifdef SIMD_SUPPORT
149       // Standard color modes (foreground/background)
150       bool use_background = (GET_OPTION(render_mode) == RENDER_MODE_BACKGROUND);
151       ascii = image_print_color_simd(resized, use_background, false, palette_chars);
152 #else
153       ascii = image_print_color(resized, palette_chars);
154 #endif
155     }
156   } else {
157     // Use grayscale/monochrome conversion with client's palette
158     ascii = image_print(resized, palette_chars);
159   }
160 
161   if (!ascii) {
162     log_error("Failed to convert image to ASCII");
163     image_destroy(resized);
164     return NULL;
165   }
166 
167   size_t ascii_len = strlen(ascii);
168   if (ascii_len == 0) {
169     log_error("ASCII conversion returned empty string (resized dimensions: %dx%d)", resized->w, resized->h);
170     SAFE_FREE(ascii);
171     image_destroy(resized);
172     return NULL;
173   }
174 
175   char *ascii_width_padded = ascii_pad_frame_width(ascii, pad_width);
176   SAFE_FREE(ascii);
177 
178   char *ascii_padded = ascii_pad_frame_height(ascii_width_padded, pad_height);
179   SAFE_FREE(ascii_width_padded);
180 
181   // Only destroy resized if we allocated it (not when using original directly)
182   image_destroy(resized);
183 
184   return ascii_padded;
185 }






References ascii_pad_frame_height(),
    ascii_pad_frame_width(), aspect_ratio(), GET_OPTION,
    image_t::h, image_clear(), image_destroy(),
    image_new(), image_print(), image_print_color(),
    image_print_color_simd(), image_resize(), log_dev,
    log_error, image_t::pixels, RENDER_MODE_BACKGROUND,
    RENDER_MODE_HALF_BLOCK, rgb_to_truecolor_halfblocks_scalar(),
    SAFE_FREE, and image_t::w.






char
  * ascii_convert_with_capabilities (image_t * original, const ssize_t
  width, const ssize_t height, const terminal_capabilities_t * caps,
  const bool use_aspect_ratio, const bool stretch, const char *
  palette_chars)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/ascii/ascii.h>


Convert image to ASCII art with terminal capability awareness.


Parameters


original Source image (must not be NULL)


width Target width in characters (must be > 0)


height Target height in characters (must be > 0)


caps Terminal capabilities structure (must not be NULL)


use_aspect_ratio Preserve aspect ratio (true to maintain proportions,
  false to fill dimensions)


stretch Stretch to fit (if use_aspect_ratio is false, true to fill
  exactly, false to fit within)


palette_chars Character palette to use (or NULL for default ASCII
  palette)


luminance_palette Luminance-to-character mapping palette (must not be
  NULL, 256 elements)


Returns


Allocated ASCII frame string (caller must free), or NULL
  on error


Converts an image to ASCII art with automatic color mode selection
    based on terminal capabilities. Automatically chooses the best color mode
    (16-color, 256-color, or truecolor) for optimal display.




CAPABILITY-AWARE CONVERSION:


    
  
  • Detects terminal color capabilities from structure
    • 
  
  • Selects optimal color mode (truecolor > 256-color > 16-color >
      monochrome)
    • 
  
  • Applies terminal character aspect ratio correction
    • 
  
  • Uses capability-specific conversion algorithms
    • 



Note


Returns NULL on error (invalid image, memory allocation
  failure).



ASCII string must be freed by caller using free().




Color mode is selected automatically based on terminal
    capabilities.




This is the recommended function for capability-aware ASCII
    conversion.




Definition at line 188 of file ascii.c.


190                                                                                      {
191 
192   if (original == NULL || caps == NULL) {
193     log_error("Invalid parameters for ascii_convert_with_capabilities");
194     return NULL;
195   }
196 
197   // Validate original image dimensions to detect corruption
198   if (original->w <= 0 || original->w > 10000 || original->h <= 0 || original->h > 10000) {
199     log_error("Invalid original image dimensions detected: w=%d, h=%d (likely corrupted)", original->w, original->h);
200     return NULL;
201   }
202 
203   if (original->pixels == NULL) {
204     log_error("Original image pixels pointer is NULL");
205     return NULL;
206   }
207 
208   // Start with the target dimensions requested by the user
209   ssize_t resized_width = width;
210   ssize_t resized_height = height;
211 
212   // Apply aspect ratio BEFORE doubling height for half-block mode
213   // This ensures the width is correct relative to the original aspect ratio
214   if (use_aspect_ratio) {
215     aspect_ratio(original->w, original->h, resized_width, resized_height, stretch, &resized_width, &resized_height);
216   }
217 
218   // Half-block mode doubles height for 2x vertical resolution (AFTER aspect ratio)
219   if (caps->render_mode == RENDER_MODE_HALF_BLOCK) {
220     resized_height = resized_height * 2;
221   }
222 
223   // Calculate padding for centering (only if client wants padding)
224   size_t pad_width = 0;
225   size_t pad_height = 0;
226 
227   log_dev("PADDING_CALC: use_aspect_ratio=%d wants_padding=%d width=%zd height=%zd resized_w=%zd resized_h=%zd",
228           use_aspect_ratio, caps->wants_padding, width, height, resized_width, resized_height);
229 
230   if (use_aspect_ratio && caps->wants_padding) {
231     ssize_t pad_width_ss = width > resized_width ? (width - resized_width) / 2 : 0;
232     pad_width = (size_t)pad_width_ss;
233 
234     ssize_t pad_height_ss = height > resized_height ? (height - resized_height) / 2 : 0;
235     // Halfblock mode uses 2 source rows per output row, so padding is halved
236     if (caps->render_mode == RENDER_MODE_HALF_BLOCK) {
237       log_dev("HALFBLOCK PADDING: before=%zd, render_mode=%d", pad_height_ss, caps->render_mode);
238       pad_height_ss /= 2;
239       log_dev("HALFBLOCK PADDING: after=%zd", pad_height_ss);
240     }
241     pad_height = (size_t)pad_height_ss;
242 
243     log_debug_every(10 * US_PER_SEC_INT,
244                     "ascii_convert_with_capabilities: width=%zd, height=%zd, resized_width=%zd, resized_height=%zd, "
245                     "pad_width=%zu, pad_height=%zu, stretch=%d, wants_padding=%d",
246                     width, height, resized_width, resized_height, pad_width, pad_height, stretch, caps->wants_padding);
247   } else if (!caps->wants_padding) {
248     log_debug_every(10 * US_PER_SEC_INT,
249                     "ascii_convert_with_capabilities: padding disabled (wants_padding=false), width=%zd, height=%zd",
250                     width, height);
251   }
252 
253   // Resize the captured frame to the aspect-correct dimensions
254   if (resized_width <= 0 || resized_height <= 0) {
255     log_error("Invalid dimensions for resize: width=%zd, height=%zd", resized_width, resized_height);
256     return NULL;
257   }
258 
259   // Validate dimensions fit in image_t's int fields before casting
260   if (resized_width > INT_MAX || resized_height > INT_MAX) {
261     log_error("Dimensions exceed INT_MAX: width=%zd, height=%zd", resized_width, resized_height);
262     return NULL;
263   }
264 
265   // PROFILING: Time image allocation and resize
266   START_TIMER("image_alloc");
267   uint64_t prof_alloc_start_ns = time_get_ns();
268 
269   image_t *resized = image_new((size_t)resized_width, (size_t)resized_height);
270   if (!resized) {
271     log_error("Failed to allocate resized image");
272     return NULL;
273   }
274 
275   image_clear(resized);
276 
277   uint64_t prof_alloc_end_ns = time_get_ns();
278   STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG_EVERY(dev, 3 * NS_PER_SEC_INT, 5 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "image_alloc",
279                            "IMAGE_ALLOC: Alloc+clear complete (%.2f ms)");
280 
281   START_TIMER("image_resize");
282   uint64_t prof_resize_start_ns = prof_alloc_end_ns;
283 
284   image_resize(original, resized);
285 
286   uint64_t prof_resize_end_ns = time_get_ns();
287   STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG_EVERY(dev, 3 * NS_PER_SEC_INT, 5 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "image_resize",
288                            "IMAGE_RESIZE: Resize complete (%.2f ms)");
289 
290   // Check original image before resize (validate dimensions to prevent integer overflow)
291   int orig_black = 0, orig_total = 0;
292   if (original->w > 0 && original->w <= 10000 && original->h > 0 && original->h <= 10000) {
293     int64_t total_pixels = (int64_t)original->w * original->h;
294     int sample_size = (total_pixels < 1000) ? (int)total_pixels : 1000;
295     for (int i = 0; i < sample_size; i++) {
296       rgb_pixel_t p = original->pixels[i];
297       orig_total++;
298       if (p.r == 0 && p.g == 0 && p.b == 0)
299         orig_black++;
300     }
301     log_debug_every(5 * NS_PER_SEC_INT,
302                     ">>> ORIGINAL image: %d/%d pixels are black (%.1f%%), first pixel=RGB(%d,%d,%d)\n", orig_black,
303                     orig_total, orig_total > 0 ? 100.0 * orig_black / orig_total : 0, original->pixels[0].r,
304                     original->pixels[0].g, original->pixels[0].b);
305   } else {
306     log_warn("Skipping debug check: corrupted image dimensions (w=%d, h=%d)", original->w, original->h);
307   }
308 
309   // PROFILING: Time ASCII print
310   uint64_t prof_print_start_ns = prof_resize_end_ns;
311 
312   // DEBUG: Log dimensions going to renderer
313   log_debug_every(10 * US_PER_SEC_INT,
314                   "ascii_convert: original=%dx%d, requested=%zdx%zd, resized=%dx%d, pad=%zux%zu (mode=%d)", original->w,
315                   original->h, width, height, resized->w, resized->h, pad_width, pad_height, caps->render_mode);
316 
317   // Check if image is all black
318   int black_pixels = 0, total_pixels = 0;
319   for (int i = 0; i < resized->w * resized->h && i < 1000; i++) {
320     rgb_pixel_t p = resized->pixels[i];
321     total_pixels++;
322     if (p.r == 0 && p.g == 0 && p.b == 0)
323       black_pixels++;
324   }
325   log_debug_every(5 * NS_PER_SEC_INT,
326                   ">>> Resized image check: %d/%d pixels are black (%.1f%%), first pixel=RGB(%d,%d,%d)\n", black_pixels,
327                   total_pixels, total_pixels > 0 ? 100.0 * black_pixels / total_pixels : 0, resized->pixels[0].r,
328                   resized->pixels[0].g, resized->pixels[0].b);
329 
330   // Use the capability-aware image printing function with client's palette
331   START_TIMER("image_print_with_capabilities");
332   char *ascii = image_print_with_capabilities(resized, caps, palette_chars);
333 
334   uint64_t prof_print_end_ns = time_get_ns();
335   STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG_EVERY(dev, 3 * NS_PER_SEC_INT, 5 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "image_print_with_capabilities",
336                            "IMAGE_PRINT: Print complete (%.2f ms)");
337 
338   uint64_t alloc_time_us = time_ns_to_us(time_elapsed_ns(prof_alloc_start_ns, prof_alloc_end_ns));
339   uint64_t resize_time_us = time_ns_to_us(time_elapsed_ns(prof_resize_start_ns, prof_resize_end_ns));
340   uint64_t print_time_us = time_ns_to_us(time_elapsed_ns(prof_print_start_ns, prof_print_end_ns));
341 
342   // PROFILING: Time padding
343   START_TIMER("ascii_padding");
344   uint64_t prof_pad_start_ns = time_get_ns();
345 
346   if (!ascii) {
347     log_error("Failed to convert image to ASCII using terminal capabilities");
348     image_destroy(resized);
349     return NULL;
350   }
351 
352   size_t ascii_len = strlen(ascii);
353   if (ascii_len == 0) {
354     log_error("Capability-aware ASCII conversion returned empty string (resized dimensions: %dx%d)", resized->w,
355               resized->h);
356     SAFE_FREE(ascii);
357     image_destroy(resized);
358     return NULL;
359   }
360 
361   char *ascii_width_padded = ascii_pad_frame_width(ascii, pad_width);
362   SAFE_FREE(ascii);
363 
364   char *ascii_padded = ascii_pad_frame_height(ascii_width_padded, pad_height);
365   SAFE_FREE(ascii_width_padded);
366 
367   uint64_t prof_pad_end_ns = time_get_ns();
368   STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG_EVERY(dev, 3 * NS_PER_SEC_INT, 2 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "ascii_padding",
369                            "ASCII_PADDING: Padding complete (%.2f ms)");
370 
371   uint64_t pad_time_ns = time_elapsed_ns(prof_pad_start_ns, prof_pad_end_ns);
372   char alloc_str[32], resize_str[32], print_str[32], pad_str[32], total_str[32];
373   time_pretty(alloc_time_us * 1000, -1, alloc_str, sizeof(alloc_str));
374   time_pretty(resize_time_us * 1000, -1, resize_str, sizeof(resize_str));
375   time_pretty(print_time_us * 1000, -1, print_str, sizeof(print_str));
376   time_pretty(pad_time_ns, -1, pad_str, sizeof(pad_str));
377   uint64_t total_time_ns = (alloc_time_us + resize_time_us + print_time_us) * 1000 + pad_time_ns;
378   time_pretty(total_time_ns, -1, total_str, sizeof(total_str));
379   log_dev("ASCII_BREAKDOWN: alloc=%s, resize=%s, print=%s, pad=%s (total=%s)", alloc_str, resize_str, print_str,
380           pad_str, total_str);
381 
382   image_destroy(resized);
383 
384   return ascii_padded;
385 }

char
  * ascii_create_grid (ascii_frame_source_t * sources, int source_count,
  int width, int height, size_t * out_size)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/ascii/ascii.h>


Create a grid layout from multiple ASCII frames.


Parameters


sources Array of frame sources (must not be NULL,
  must have source_count elements)


source_count Number of frame sources (must be > 0)


width Grid width in characters per frame (must be > 0)


height Grid height in characters per frame (must be > 0)


out_size Pointer to store output size in bytes (can be NULL)


Returns


Allocated grid frame string (caller must free), or NULL
  on error


Combines multiple ASCII frames into a single grid layout. Arranges
    frames in a grid pattern suitable for multi-user display. Each frame is
    positioned in the grid according to its index in the sources array.




GRID LAYOUT:


    
  
  • Frames are arranged in grid pattern (rows and columns)
    • 
  
  • Each frame has specified width and height in characters
    • 
  
  • Grid dimensions are calculated based on source_count
    • 
  
  • Output frame contains all frames arranged in grid
    • 



Note


Returns NULL on error (invalid sources, memory allocation
  failure).



Grid frame string must be freed by caller using free().




Frame sources must have valid frame_data and frame_size.




Grid layout is useful for multi-user video display.




Example








ascii_frame_source_t sources[4];
// Initialize sources...
char *grid = ascii_create_grid(sources, 4, 80, 40, NULL);
if (grid) {


    ascii_write(grid);


    free(grid);
}





Creates a grid layout from multiple ASCII frame sources with | and
    _ separators.




Parameters: sources Array of ASCII frame sources to combine
    source_count Number of sources in the array width Target width of the output
    grid height Target height of the output grid out_size Output parameter for
    the size of the returned buffer




Returns: A newly allocated, null-terminated string containing the
    grid layout, or NULL on error. Caller must free the returned buffer.


Definition at line 600 of file ascii.c.


600                                                                                                                   {
601   if (!sources || source_count <= 0 || width <= 0 || height <= 0 || !out_size) {
602     return NULL;
603   }
604 
605   // If no sources, return empty frame
606 
607   // If only one source, center it properly to maintain aspect ratio and look good
608   if (source_count == 1) {
609     // Create a frame of the target size filled with spaces
610     // Check for integer overflow before multiplication
611     size_t w = (size_t)width;
612     size_t h = (size_t)height;
613     size_t w_times_h;
614     if (checked_size_mul(w, h, &w_times_h) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
615       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "ascii_create_grid: dimensions would overflow: %dx%d", width, height);
616       return NULL;
617     }
618 
619     size_t w_times_h_plus_h;
620     if (checked_size_add(w_times_h, h, &w_times_h_plus_h) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
621       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "ascii_create_grid: buffer size would overflow: %dx%d", width, height);
622       return NULL;
623     }
624 
625     size_t target_size;
626     if (checked_size_add(w_times_h_plus_h, 1, &target_size) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
627       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "ascii_create_grid: buffer size would overflow: %dx%d", width, height);
628       return NULL;
629     }
630     char *result;
631     result = SAFE_MALLOC(target_size, char *);
632     SAFE_MEMSET(result, target_size, ' ', target_size - 1);
633     result[target_size - 1] = '\0';
634 
635     // Add newlines at the end of each row
636     for (int row = 0; row < height; row++) {
637       result[row * (width + 1) + width] = '\n';
638     }
639 
640     // Copy the source frame into the result, line by line, centering it
641     // Handle NULL frame_data gracefully
642     const char *src_data = sources[0].frame_data;
643     int src_pos = 0;
644     int src_size = (int)sources[0].frame_size;
645 
646     // If source data is NULL or empty, just return the empty frame
647     if (!src_data || src_size <= 0) {
648       *out_size = target_size - 1; // Don't count null terminator
649       return result;
650     }
651 
652     // Count lines in source to calculate vertical padding
653     int src_lines = 0;
654     for (int i = 0; i < src_size; i++) {
655       if (src_data[i] == '\n')
656         src_lines++;
657     }
658 
659     int v_padding = (height - src_lines) / 2;
660     if (v_padding < 0)
661       v_padding = 0;
662 
663     int dst_row = v_padding;
664     src_pos = 0;
665 
666     while (src_pos < src_size && dst_row < height) {
667       // Find end of current line in source
668       int line_start = src_pos;
669       int line_len = 0;
670       while (src_pos < src_size && src_data[src_pos] != '\n') {
671         line_len++;
672         src_pos++;
673       }
674 
675       // Calculate visual width of the line (excluding ANSI escape sequences)
676       int visual_line_width = ansi_visual_width(&src_data[line_start], line_len);
677 
678       // Calculate horizontal padding to center the line based on visual width
679       int h_padding = (width - visual_line_width) / 2;
680       if (h_padding < 0)
681         h_padding = 0;
682 
683       // Copy line to result with padding
684       // Use size_t for position calculation to prevent integer underflow
685       size_t row_offset = (size_t)dst_row * (size_t)(width + 1);
686       size_t dst_pos = row_offset + (size_t)h_padding;
687       int max_visual_width = width - h_padding;
688       // Truncate to visual width while preserving complete ANSI sequences
689       int copy_len = ansi_truncate_to_visual_width(&src_data[line_start], line_len, max_visual_width);
690 
691       if (copy_len > 0 && dst_pos + (size_t)copy_len < target_size) {
692         SAFE_MEMCPY(&result[dst_pos], target_size - dst_pos, &src_data[line_start], (size_t)copy_len);
693       }
694 
695       // Skip newline in source
696       if (src_pos < src_size && src_data[src_pos] == '\n') {
697         src_pos++;
698       }
699 
700       dst_row++;
701     }
702 
703     *out_size = target_size - 1; // Don't count null terminator
704     return result;
705   }
706 
707   // Multiple sources: create grid layout
708   // Calculate grid dimensions that maximize the use of terminal space
709   // Character aspect ratio: terminal chars are typically ~2x taller than wide
710   float char_aspect = 2.0f;
711 
712   int grid_cols, grid_rows;
713   float best_score = -1.0f;
714   int best_cols = 1;
715   int best_rows = source_count;
716 
717   // Try all possible grid configurations
718   for (int test_cols = 1; test_cols <= source_count; test_cols++) {
719     int test_rows = (int)ceil((double)source_count / test_cols);
720 
721     // Skip configurations with too many empty cells
722     int empty_cells = (test_cols * test_rows) - source_count;
723     if (empty_cells > source_count / 2)
724       continue; // Don't waste more than 50% space
725 
726     // Calculate the size each cell would have
727     int cell_width = (width - (test_cols - 1)) / test_cols;   // -1 per separator
728     int cell_height = (height - (test_rows - 1)) / test_rows; // -1 per separator
729 
730     // Skip if cells would be too small
731     if (cell_width < 10 || cell_height < 3)
732       continue;
733 
734     // Calculate the aspect ratio of each cell (accounting for char aspect)
735     float cell_aspect = ((float)cell_width / (float)cell_height) / char_aspect;
736 
737     // Score based on how close to square (1:1) each video cell would be
738     // This naturally adapts to any terminal size
739     float aspect_score = 1.0f - fabsf(logf(cell_aspect)); // log makes it symmetric around 1
740     if (aspect_score < 0)
741       aspect_score = 0;
742 
743     // Bonus for better space utilization
744     float utilization = (float)source_count / (float)(test_cols * test_rows);
745 
746     // For 2 clients specifically, heavily weight the aspect score
747     // This makes 2 clients naturally go horizontal on wide terminals and vertical on tall ones
748     float total_score;
749     if (source_count == 2) {
750       // For 2 clients, we want the layout that gives the most square-ish cells
751       total_score = aspect_score * 0.9f + utilization * 0.1f;
752     } else {
753       // For 3+ clients, balance aspect ratio with space utilization
754       total_score = aspect_score * 0.7f + utilization * 0.3f;
755     }
756 
757     // Small bonus for simpler grids (prefer 2x2 over 3x1, etc.)
758     if (test_cols == test_rows) {
759       total_score += 0.05f; // Slight preference for square grids
760     }
761 
762     if (total_score > best_score) {
763       best_score = total_score;
764       best_cols = test_cols;
765       best_rows = test_rows;
766     }
767   }
768 
769   grid_cols = best_cols;
770   grid_rows = best_rows;
771 
772   // Calculate dimensions for each cell (leave 1 char for separators)
773   int cell_width = (width - (grid_cols - 1)) / grid_cols;
774   int cell_height = (height - (grid_rows - 1)) / grid_rows;
775 
776   if (cell_width < 10 || cell_height < 3) {
777     // Too small for grid layout, just use first source
778     char *result;
779     result = SAFE_MALLOC(sources[0].frame_size + 1, char *);
780     if (sources[0].frame_data && sources[0].frame_size > 0) {
781       SAFE_MEMCPY(result, sources[0].frame_size + 1, sources[0].frame_data, sources[0].frame_size);
782       result[sources[0].frame_size] = '\0';
783       *out_size = sources[0].frame_size;
784     } else {
785       // Handle NULL or empty frame data
786       result[0] = '\0';
787       *out_size = 0;
788     }
789     return result;
790   }
791 
792   // Allocate mixed frame buffer
793   // Check for integer overflow before multiplication
794   size_t w_sz = (size_t)width;
795   size_t h_sz = (size_t)height;
796   if (w_sz > SIZE_MAX / h_sz) {
797     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "ascii_create_grid: dimensions would overflow: %dx%d", width, height);
798     return NULL;
799   }
800   size_t mixed_size = w_sz * h_sz + h_sz + 1; // +1 for null terminator, +height for newlines
801   char *mixed_frame;
802   mixed_frame = SAFE_MALLOC(mixed_size, char *);
803 
804   // Initialize mixed frame with spaces
805   SAFE_MEMSET(mixed_frame, mixed_size, ' ', mixed_size - 1);
806   mixed_frame[mixed_size - 1] = '\0';
807 
808   // Add newlines at the end of each row
809   for (int row = 0; row < height; row++) {
810     mixed_frame[row * (width + 1) + width] = '\n';
811   }
812 
813   // Place each video source in the grid
814   for (int src = 0; src < source_count; src++) {
815     int grid_row = src / grid_cols;
816     int grid_col = src % grid_cols;
817 
818     // Calculate position in mixed frame
819     int start_row = grid_row * (cell_height + 1); // +1 for separator
820     int start_col = grid_col * (cell_width + 1);  // +1 for separator
821 
822     // Parse source frame line by line and place in grid
823     const char *src_data = sources[src].frame_data;
824     int src_row = 0;
825     int src_pos = 0;
826 
827     while (src_pos < (int)sources[src].frame_size && src_row < cell_height && start_row + src_row < height) {
828       // Find end of current line in source
829       int line_start = src_pos;
830       while (src_pos < (int)sources[src].frame_size && src_data[src_pos] != '\n') {
831         src_pos++;
832       }
833       int line_len = src_pos - line_start;
834 
835       // Copy line to mixed frame (truncate if too long)
836       // Truncate to visual cell width while preserving complete ANSI sequences
837       int copy_len = ansi_truncate_to_visual_width(src_data + line_start, line_len, cell_width);
838       // Check that visual width of truncated line fits
839       int truncated_visual_width = ansi_visual_width(src_data + line_start, copy_len);
840       if (copy_len > 0 && start_col + truncated_visual_width <= width) {
841         int mixed_pos = (start_row + src_row) * (width + 1) + start_col;
842         SAFE_MEMCPY(mixed_frame + mixed_pos, mixed_size - (size_t)mixed_pos, src_data + line_start, (size_t)copy_len);
843       }
844 
845       // Move to next line
846       if (src_pos < (int)sources[src].frame_size && src_data[src_pos] == '\n') {
847         src_pos++;
848       }
849       src_row++;
850     }
851 
852     // Draw separators with bounds checking to prevent buffer overflow
853     if (grid_col < grid_cols - 1 && start_col + cell_width < width) {
854       // Vertical separator
855       for (int row = start_row; row < start_row + cell_height && row < height; row++) {
856         size_t idx = (size_t)row * (size_t)(width + 1) + (size_t)(start_col + cell_width);
857         if (idx < mixed_size - 1) { // -1 to preserve null terminator
858           mixed_frame[idx] = '|';
859         }
860       }
861     }
862 
863     if (grid_row < grid_rows - 1 && start_row + cell_height < height) {
864       // Horizontal separator
865       for (int col = start_col; col < start_col + cell_width && col < width; col++) {
866         size_t idx = (size_t)(start_row + cell_height) * (size_t)(width + 1) + (size_t)col;
867         if (idx < mixed_size - 1) { // -1 to preserve null terminator
868           mixed_frame[idx] = '_';
869         }
870       }
871       // Corner character where separators meet
872       if (grid_col < grid_cols - 1 && start_col + cell_width < width) {
873         size_t idx = (size_t)(start_row + cell_height) * (size_t)(width + 1) + (size_t)(start_col + cell_width);
874         if (idx < mixed_size - 1) { // -1 to preserve null terminator
875           mixed_frame[idx] = '+';
876         }
877       }
878     }
879   }
880 
881   *out_size = strlen(mixed_frame);
882   return mixed_frame;
883 }

    ascii_frame_source_t::frame_data,
    ascii_frame_source_t::frame_size, SAFE_MALLOC,

char
  * ascii_pad_frame_height (const char * frame, size_t pad_top)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/ascii/ascii.h>


Add blank lines (vertical padding) to center a frame
  vertically.


Parameters


frame ASCII frame string (must
  not be NULL)


pad_top Number of blank lines to add at top (must be >= 0)


Returns


Allocated padded frame string (caller must free with
  free()), or NULL on error


Adds blank lines (vertical padding) to the top of an ASCII frame.
    Useful for centering frames vertically or creating margins. Blank lines are
    added at the top of the frame.




Note


Returns NULL on error (invalid frame, memory allocation
  failure).



Padded frame string must be freed by caller using free().




Frame width is preserved (characters per line unchanged).




Frame height is increased by 'pad_top' lines.




Adds vertical padding (blank lines) to center a frame
  vertically.




Parameters: frame The input ASCII frame to pad vertically. pad_top
    Number of blank lines to add at the top.




Returns: A newly allocated, null-terminated string with vertical
    padding, or NULL if frame is NULL.




NOTE: Uses plain newlines instead of ANSI escape sequences to
    support both TTY and piped/redirected output. TTY flicker prevention is
    handled by the display layer (e.g., display.c) when appropriate.


Definition at line 900 of file ascii.c.


900                                                                 {
901   if (!frame) {
902     return NULL;
903   }
904 
905   if (pad_top == 0) {
906     // Nothing to do; return a copy because the caller knows to free() the value.
907     size_t orig_len = strlen(frame);
908     char *copy;
909     copy = SAFE_MALLOC(orig_len + 1, char *);
910     SAFE_MEMCPY(copy, orig_len + 1, frame, orig_len + 1);
911     return copy;
912   }
913 
914   // Calculate buffer size needed
915   // Each padding row needs: 1 newline character per padding row
916   size_t frame_len = strlen(frame);
917   size_t top_padding_len = pad_top; // 1 newline per padding row
918   size_t total_len = top_padding_len + frame_len;
919 
920   char *buffer;
921   buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(total_len + 1, char *);
922 
923   char *position = buffer;
924 
925   // Add top padding with plain newlines
926   // Use plain newlines instead of ANSI escape sequences so the output works
927   // when redirected to pipes or files, not just TTY
928   for (size_t i = 0; i < pad_top; i++) {
929     *position++ = '\n';
930   }
931 
932   // Copy the original frame
933   size_t remaining = frame_len + 1;
934   SAFE_MEMCPY(position, remaining, frame, frame_len);
935   position += frame_len;
936   *position = '\0';
937 
938   return buffer;
939 }

#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/ascii/ascii.h>


Add leading spaces (left-padding) to each line of a frame.


Parameters


frame ASCII frame string (must
  not be NULL)


pad Number of spaces to pad on left (must be >= 0)


Returns


Allocated padded frame string (caller must free with
  free()), or NULL on error


Adds leading spaces (left-padding) to each line of an ASCII frame.
    Useful for centering frames horizontally or creating margins. Each line in
    the frame is padded with the specified number of spaces.




Note


Returns NULL on error (invalid frame, memory allocation
  failure).



Padded frame string must be freed by caller using free().




Frame height is preserved (number of lines unchanged).




Frame width is increased by 'pad' characters.

459   }
460 
461   if (pad_left == 0) {
462     // Nothing to do; return a copy so the caller can free it safely without
463     // worrying about the original allocation strategy.
464     size_t orig_len = strlen(frame);
465     char *copy;
466     copy = SAFE_MALLOC(orig_len + 1, char *);
467     SAFE_MEMCPY(copy, orig_len + 1, frame, orig_len + 1);
468     return copy;
469   }
470 
471   // Count how many visual rows we have (lines terminated by '\n') to determine
472   // the final buffer size.

475   while (*char_in_frame) {

478     }
479     char_in_frame++;
480   }
481

486

489

492   const char *src = frame;
493   char *position = buffer;
494 
495   while (*src) {
496     if (at_line_start) {
497       // Insert the requested amount of spaces in front of every visual row.
498       size_t remaining = (size_t)((ptrdiff_t)(buffer + total_len + 1) - (ptrdiff_t)position);
499       SAFE_MEMSET(position, remaining, ' ', (size_t)pad_left);
500       position += pad_left;

502     }
503

505 
506     if (*src == '\n') {

508     }
509 
510     src++;
511   }
512 
513   *position = '\0';
514   return buffer;

Note

  conversion.



Must call ascii_read_destroy() when done.

asciichat_error_t ascii_write (const char * frame)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/ascii/ascii.h>


Write ASCII frame to terminal.


Parameters


frame ASCII frame string to write
  (must not be NULL)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Writes an ASCII frame string to the terminal output. Frame string
    may contain ANSI escape sequences for color output and cursor positioning.
    Output is written to the file descriptor configured during
    ascii_write_init().




Note


Frame string should be null-terminated.



ANSI escape sequences in frame string are processed by
  terminal.




Output is written immediately (no buffering).




Definition at line 390 of file ascii.c.


390                                                  {
391   if (frame == NULL) {
392     log_warn("Attempted to write NULL frame");
393     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
394   }
395 
396   // Only reset cursor if output is connected to a TTY (not piped/redirected)
397   if (terminal_should_use_control_sequences(STDOUT_FILENO)) {
398     cursor_reset(STDOUT_FILENO);
399   }
400 
401   size_t frame_len = strlen(frame);
402   // Write all frame data with automatic retry on transient errors
403   platform_write_all(STDOUT_FILENO, frame, frame_len);
404 
405   // Flush C stdio buffer and terminal to ensure piped output is written immediately
406   (void)fflush(stdout);
407   terminal_flush(STDOUT_FILENO);
408 
409   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
410 }

void
  ascii_write_destroy (int fd, bool reset_terminal)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/ascii/ascii.h>


Destroy ASCII write subsystem.


Parameters


fd File descriptor that was used for writing (must
  be valid)


reset_terminal Whether to reset terminal on cleanup (true to restore
  state, false to preserve)


Cleans up the ASCII write subsystem and restores terminal state.
    Restores cursor visibility, clears screen, and resets terminal attributes if
    reset_terminal is true.




TERMINAL CLEANUP:


    
  
  • Restores cursor visibility (if reset_terminal is true)
    • 
  
  • Resets terminal attributes (if reset_terminal is true)
    • 
  
  • Clears screen (if reset_terminal is true)
    • 
  
  • Restores terminal to default state
    • 



Note


Safe to call multiple times (no-op after first call).



Reset terminal restores terminal to default state.




After cleanup, terminal output functions may fail until
    re-initialized.




Definition at line 412 of file ascii.c.


412                                                       {
413 #if PLATFORM_WINDOWS
414   (void)fd; // Unused on Windows - terminal operations use stdout directly
415 #endif
416   // console_clear(fd);
417   // cursor_reset(fd);
418   // Only restore terminal state if:
419   // 1. reset_terminal is true (caller wants terminal restore)
420   // 2. terminal_should_use_control_sequences() confirms it's safe (TTY, not snapshot, not testing)
421   if (reset_terminal && terminal_should_use_control_sequences(fd)) {
422     // Show cursor using platform abstraction
423     if (terminal_cursor_show() != 0) {
424       log_warn("Failed to show cursor");
425     }
426 
427     // Re-enable echo using platform abstraction
428     if (terminal_set_echo(true) != 0) {
429       log_warn("Failed to re-enable echo");
430     }
431   }
432   log_debug("ASCII writer destroyed");
433 }

Initialize ASCII write subsystem.

Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Initializes the ASCII write subsystem for terminal output.
    Configures terminal for ASCII art display including cursor hiding, screen
    clearing, and terminal state management.




TERMINAL INITIALIZATION:


    
  
  • Hides cursor (if reset_terminal is true)
    • 
  
  • Clears screen (if reset_terminal is true)
    • 
  
  • Configures terminal for raw output
    • 
  
  • Prepares terminal for ASCII art display
    •

Reset terminal clears screen and hides cursor for clean
  display.




File descriptor is used for terminal output (stdout, stderr,
    etc.).

48

50   // 1. reset_terminal is true (caller wants terminal reset)
51   // 2. terminal_should_use_control_sequences() confirms it's safe (TTY, not snapshot, not testing)
52   if (reset_terminal && terminal_should_use_control_sequences(fd)) {
53     console_clear(fd);
54     cursor_reset(fd);
55 
56     // Disable echo using platform abstraction
57     if (terminal_set_echo(false) != 0) {
58       log_error("Failed to disable echo for fd %d", fd);
59       return ERROR_TERMINAL;
60     }
61     // Hide cursor using platform abstraction

66   log_dev("ASCII writer initialized");

void
  build_utf8_luminance_cache (const char * ascii_chars, utf8_char_t
  cache[256])


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/ascii/common.h>


Build UTF-8 luminance cache for all 256 levels.


Definition at line 387 of file
  video/ascii/common.c.


387                                                                                  {
388   if (!ascii_chars || !cache)
389     return;
390 
391   // Parse characters
392   typedef struct {
393     const char *start;
394     int byte_len;
395   } char_info_t;
396 
397   char_info_t char_infos[256];
398   int char_count = 0;
399   const char *p = ascii_chars;
400 
401   while (*p && char_count < 255) {
402     char_infos[char_count].start = p;
403 
404     if ((*p & 0xE0) == 0xC0) {
405       char_infos[char_count].byte_len = 2;
406       p += 2;
407     } else if ((*p & 0xF0) == 0xE0) {
408       char_infos[char_count].byte_len = 3;
409       p += 3;
410     } else if ((*p & 0xF8) == 0xF0) {
411       char_infos[char_count].byte_len = 4;
412       p += 4;
413     } else {
414       // ASCII characters (0x00-0x7F) and invalid sequences: treat as single byte
415       char_infos[char_count].byte_len = 1;
416       p++;
417     }
418     char_count++;
419   }
420 
421   // Handle empty string case
422   if (char_count == 0)
423     return;
424 
425   // Build 256-entry cache
426   for (int i = 0; i < 256; i++) {
427     int char_idx = char_count > 1 ? (i * (char_count - 1) + 127) / 255 : 0;
428     if (char_idx >= char_count)
429       char_idx = char_count - 1;
430 
431     cache[i].byte_len = char_infos[char_idx].byte_len;
432     memcpy(cache[i].utf8_bytes, char_infos[char_idx].start, char_infos[char_idx].byte_len);
433     if (cache[i].byte_len < 4) {
434       cache[i].utf8_bytes[cache[i].byte_len] = '\0';
435     }
436   }
437 }

    utf8_char_t::utf8_bytes.

446 
447   // Parse characters (same as above)
448   typedef struct {
449     const char *start;
450     int byte_len;
451   } char_info_t;
452 
453   char_info_t char_infos[256];
454   int char_count = 0;
455   const char *p = ascii_chars;
456 
457   while (*p && char_count < 255) {
458     char_infos[char_count].start = p;
459 
460     if ((*p & 0xE0) == 0xC0) {
461       char_infos[char_count].byte_len = 2;
462       p += 2;
463     } else if ((*p & 0xF0) == 0xE0) {
464       char_infos[char_count].byte_len = 3;
465       p += 3;
466     } else if ((*p & 0xF8) == 0xF0) {
467       char_infos[char_count].byte_len = 4;
468       p += 4;
469     } else {
470       // ASCII characters (0x00-0x7F) and invalid sequences: treat as single byte
471       char_infos[char_count].byte_len = 1;
472       p++;
473     }
474     char_count++;
475   }
476 
477   // Handle empty string case
478   if (char_count == 0)
479     return;
480 
481   // Build 64-entry cache and index ramp
482   for (int i = 0; i < 64; i++) {
483     int char_idx = char_count > 1 ? (i * (char_count - 1) + 31) / 63 : 0;
484     if (char_idx >= char_count)
485       char_idx = char_count - 1;
486 
487     // Store character index for SIMD lookup
488     char_index_ramp[i] = (uint8_t)char_idx;
489 
490     // Cache UTF-8 character
491     cache64[i].byte_len = char_infos[char_idx].byte_len;
492     memcpy(cache64[i].utf8_bytes, char_infos[char_idx].start, char_infos[char_idx].byte_len);
493     if (cache64[i].byte_len < 4) {
494       cache64[i].utf8_bytes[cache64[i].byte_len] = '\0';
495     }
496   }
497 }






References utf8_char_t::byte_len, and
    utf8_char_t::utf8_bytes.

void
  color_filter_calculate_rainbow (float time, uint8_t * r, uint8_t
  * g, uint8_t * b)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/color_filter.h>


Calculate rainbow color from time (3.5s cycle).


Parameters


time Current time in seconds


r Output red component (0-255)


g Output green component (0-255)


b Output blue component (0-255)


Centralized rainbow color calculation used by both --matrix and
    --color-filter rainbow. Cycles through full RGB spectrum over 3.5
  seconds.




Calculate rainbow color from time Cycles through full spectrum
    over 3.5 seconds This is the centralized rainbow implementation used by both
    matrix and color filter


Definition at line 169 of file color_filter.c.


169                                                                                     {
170   // Cycle period: 3.5 seconds
171   const float cycle_period = 3.5f;
172 
173   // Normalize time to [0, 1] range within cycle
174   float phase = fmodf(time, cycle_period) / cycle_period;
175 
176   // Convert to hue angle [0, 360)
177   float hue = phase * 360.0f;
178 
179   // HSV to RGB conversion (S=1, V=1 for full saturation and brightness)
180   // Hue divided into 6 segments
181   float h = hue / 60.0f;
182   int i = (int)floorf(h);
183   float f = h - (float)i;
184 
185   // Calculate RGB based on hue segment
186   float q = 1.0f - f;
187   float t = f;
188 
189   switch (i % 6) {
190   case 0:
191     *r = 255;
192     *g = (uint8_t)(t * 255.0f + 0.5f);
193     *b = 0;
194     break; // Red to Yellow
195   case 1:
196     *r = (uint8_t)(q * 255.0f + 0.5f);
197     *g = 255;
198     *b = 0;
199     break; // Yellow to Green
200   case 2:
201     *r = 0;
202     *g = 255;
203     *b = (uint8_t)(t * 255.0f + 0.5f);
204     break; // Green to Cyan
205   case 3:
206     *r = 0;
207     *g = (uint8_t)(q * 255.0f + 0.5f);
208     *b = 255;
209     break; // Cyan to Blue
210   case 4:
211     *r = (uint8_t)(t * 255.0f + 0.5f);
212     *g = 0;
213     *b = 255;
214     break; // Blue to Magenta
215   case 5:
216     *r = 255;
217     *g = 0;
218     *b = (uint8_t)(q * 255.0f + 0.5f);
219     break; // Magenta to Red
220   default:
221     *r = 255;
222     *g = 0;
223     *b = 0;
224     break; // Fallback to red
225   }
226 
227   // Ensure minimum perceived brightness by boosting luminance
228   // Pure blue (0,0,255) has luminance ~18, which appears very dark
229   // We need minimum luminance of ~120 for bright, vibrant colors
230   const float min_luminance = 120.0f;
231 
232   // Calculate current luminance using ITU-R BT.709 coefficients
233   float luminance = 0.2126f * *r + 0.7152f * *g + 0.0722f * *b;
234 
235   if (luminance < min_luminance) {
236     // Boost luminance by adding white (increasing all channels proportionally)
237     // This desaturates the color but makes it much brighter
238     float boost = (min_luminance - luminance) / 3.0f;
239     *r = (uint8_t)fminf(255.0f, *r + boost);
240     *g = (uint8_t)fminf(255.0f, *g + boost);
241     *b = (uint8_t)fminf(255.0f, *b + boost);
242   }
243 }






References b.


Referenced by apply_color_filter(),
    digital_rain_apply(), and rainbow_replace_ansi_colors().

char
  * digital_rain_apply (digital_rain_t * rain, const char * frame, float
  delta_time)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/anim/digital_rain.h>


Apply digital rain effect to ASCII frame.


Parameters


rain Digital rain context (must not be NULL)


frame Input ASCII frame (must not be NULL, must be
  null-terminated)


delta_time Time elapsed since last frame in seconds


Returns


Allocated frame with digital rain effect applied, or NULL
  on error


Applies the digital rain effect to an ASCII frame by analyzing
    character positions and injecting ANSI color codes to modulate brightness.
    The effect:





  
1.

  
Parses the frame to identify grid positions

  
2.

  
Calculates per-cell brightness using sawtooth wave pattern

  
3.

  
Detects cursor positions (brightness increases)

  
4.

  
Injects ANSI color codes to darken/brighten characters

  
5.

  
Returns new frame with effect applied




FRAME FORMAT:


    
  
  • Input can be plain ASCII or contain existing ANSI codes
    • 
  
  • Output contains ANSI color codes for brightness modulation
    • 
  
  • Frame dimensions must match num_columns x num_rows
    • 
  
  • Newlines are preserved in output
    • 



Note


Caller must free returned frame string



Input frame is not modified




Works with both color and monochrome frames




Preserves existing ANSI codes (may override colors)




Definition at line 369 of file digital_rain.c.


369                                                                                     {
370   if (!rain || !frame) {
371     log_error("digital_rain_apply: NULL parameter");
372     return NULL;
373   }
374 
375   // Update time
376   rain->time += delta_time * rain->animation_speed;
377   float sim_time = rain->time;
378 
379   // Update rainbow color if rainbow mode is enabled
380   if (rain->rainbow_mode) {
381     color_filter_calculate_rainbow(sim_time, &rain->color_r, &rain->color_g, &rain->color_b);
382   }
383 
384   // Allocate output buffer (input size + overhead for ANSI codes)
385   // Estimate: each character might need up to 20 bytes for ANSI codes
386   size_t input_len = strlen(frame);
387   size_t output_capacity = input_len * 20 + 1024;
388   char *output = SAFE_MALLOC(output_capacity, char *);
389   if (!output) {
390     log_error("digital_rain_apply: failed to allocate output buffer");
391     return NULL;
392   }
393 
394   const char *src = frame;
395   char *dst = output;
396   size_t remaining = output_capacity;
397 
398   int col = 0;
399   int row = 0;
400 
401   while (*src && remaining > 100) {
402     // Try to parse and modify ANSI color sequences
403     if (*src == '\033') {
404       int r, g, b;
405       bool is_foreground;
406       const char *after = parse_ansi_color(src, &r, &g, &b, &is_foreground);
407 
408       if (after) {
409         // Found a color sequence - calculate brightness for this position
410         float brightness = get_rain_brightness(rain, col, row, sim_time);
411         float brightness_below = get_rain_brightness(rain, col, row + 1, sim_time);
412         bool is_cursor = brightness > brightness_below;
413 
414         // Blend with previous brightness for smooth transitions
415         if (!rain->first_frame && row < rain->num_rows && col < rain->num_columns) {
416           size_t idx = (size_t)row * (size_t)rain->num_columns + (size_t)col;
417           float prev_brightness = rain->previous_brightness[idx];
418           brightness = prev_brightness + (brightness - prev_brightness) * rain->brightness_decay;
419           rain->previous_brightness[idx] = brightness;
420         } else if (row < rain->num_rows && col < rain->num_columns) {
421           size_t idx = (size_t)row * (size_t)rain->num_columns + (size_t)col;
422           rain->previous_brightness[idx] = brightness;
423         }
424 
425         // Generate modulated color
426         char ansi_buf[32];
427         int ansi_len =
428             generate_modulated_color(ansi_buf, sizeof(ansi_buf), r, g, b, brightness, is_foreground, is_cursor);
429 
430         if ((size_t)ansi_len < remaining) {
431           memcpy(dst, ansi_buf, (size_t)ansi_len);
432           dst += ansi_len;
433           remaining -= (size_t)ansi_len;
434         }
435 
436         src = after;
437         continue;
438       } else {
439         // Not a color sequence - copy as-is
440         const char *after_skip = skip_ansi_sequence(src);
441         size_t ansi_len = (size_t)(after_skip - src);
442         if (ansi_len < remaining) {
443           memcpy(dst, src, ansi_len);
444           dst += ansi_len;
445           remaining -= ansi_len;
446           src = after_skip;
447         } else {
448           break;
449         }
450         continue;
451       }
452     }
453 
454     // Handle newline
455     if (*src == '\n') {
456       *dst++ = *src++;
457       remaining--;
458       row++;
459       col = 0;
460       continue;
461     }
462 
463     // Copy regular characters - inject color for non-colored characters
464     if (remaining > 0) {
465       // Calculate brightness for this position
466       float brightness = get_rain_brightness(rain, col, row, sim_time);
467       float brightness_below = get_rain_brightness(rain, col, row + 1, sim_time);
468       bool is_cursor = brightness > brightness_below;
469 
470       // Blend with previous brightness
471       if (!rain->first_frame && row < rain->num_rows && col < rain->num_columns) {
472         size_t idx = (size_t)row * (size_t)rain->num_columns + (size_t)col;
473         float prev_brightness = rain->previous_brightness[idx];
474         brightness = prev_brightness + (brightness - prev_brightness) * rain->brightness_decay;
475         rain->previous_brightness[idx] = brightness;
476       } else if (row < rain->num_rows && col < rain->num_columns) {
477         size_t idx = (size_t)row * (size_t)rain->num_columns + (size_t)col;
478         rain->previous_brightness[idx] = brightness;
479       }
480 
481       // For characters without explicit color codes, use the rain's default color
482       char ansi_buf[32];
483       int ansi_len = generate_modulated_color(ansi_buf, sizeof(ansi_buf), rain->color_r, rain->color_g, rain->color_b,
484                                               brightness, true, is_cursor);
485 
486       if ((size_t)ansi_len < remaining) {
487         memcpy(dst, ansi_buf, (size_t)ansi_len);
488         dst += ansi_len;
489         remaining -= (size_t)ansi_len;
490       }
491 
492       // Decode UTF-8 character to get byte length
493       uint32_t codepoint;
494       int utf8_len = utf8_decode((const uint8_t *)src, &codepoint);
495       if (utf8_len < 0) {
496         // Invalid UTF-8, treat as single byte
497         utf8_len = 1;
498       }
499 
500       // Copy all bytes of the UTF-8 character
501       for (int i = 0; i < utf8_len && *src && remaining > 0; i++) {
502         *dst++ = *src++;
503         remaining--;
504       }
505 
506       // Only increment column once per character (not per byte)
507       col++;
508     } else {
509       break;
510     }
511   }
512 
513   // Null-terminate
514   if (remaining > 0) {
515     *dst = '\0';
516   } else {
517     output[output_capacity - 1] = '\0';
518   }
519 
520   rain->first_frame = false;
521   return output;
522 }
