void
  emit_rep (outbuf_t * ob, uint32_t extra)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/ascii/output_buffer.h>


Emit run-length encoded sequence.


Parameters


ob Output buffer structure (must not be NULL)


extra Additional characters to repeat (must be > 0)


Emits an ANSI run-length encoding sequence that repeats the last
    character in the buffer 'extra' times. Format: ESC[extram (where 'extra' is
    the repeat count and the last character is repeated).




Note


This function repeats the character at buffer[len-1].



Useful for compressing repeated characters (spaces, blocks,
  etc.).




Only profitable for runs exceeding threshold (see
    rep_is_profitable()).




Example








ob_putc(ob, ' ');  // Append space
if (rep_is_profitable(50)) {


    emit_rep(ob, 49);  // Repeat space 49 more times (total 50)
}

158     return;
159   // ESC [ extra b
160   ob_putc(ob, 0x1b);
161   ob_putc(ob, '[');
162   ob_u32(ob, extra);
163   ob_putc(ob, 'b');
164 }

Emit 256-color background ANSI sequence.

color_idx 256-color palette index (0-255)


Emits ANSI escape sequence for 256-color background color. Format:

    palette for 256-color mode.




Note

Palette indices 0-15 are standard ANSI colors.




Palette indices 16-255 are extended palette.

void
  emit_set_bg (outbuf_t * ob, uint8_t r, uint8_t g,
  uint8_t b)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/ascii/output_buffer.h>


Emit background color sequence (auto-select mode).


Parameters


ob Output buffer structure (must not be NULL)


r Red component (0-255)


g Green component (0-255)


b Blue component (0-255)


Emits background color ANSI sequence using the optimal color mode
    based on terminal capabilities. Automatically selects:


    
  
  • Truecolor mode (if supported): 24-bit RGB
    • 
  
  • 256-color mode (if supported): Quantized to 256-color palette
    • 
  
  • 16-color mode (fallback): Quantized to standard ANSI colors
    • 



Note


Color mode is selected based on
  terminal_capabilities_t configuration.



This function uses the best available color mode for the
  terminal.




Color quantization is applied when truecolor is not available.

207   ob_write(ob, "48;2;", 5);
208   ob_u8(ob, r);
209   ob_putc(ob, ';');
210   ob_u8(ob, g);
211   ob_putc(ob, ';');
212   ob_u8(ob, b);

char
  * get_lum_palette (void )


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/ascii/ascii.h>


Get luminance palette for character mapping.


Returns


Pointer to luminance palette array (256 elements, do not
  free)


Returns the default luminance-to-character mapping palette. The
    palette contains 256 characters ordered by luminance (dark to light) for
    mapping pixel brightness values (0-255) to ASCII characters.




Note


Returns pointer to static array (do not free).



Palette has 256 elements (one for each luminance level).




Palette is used for monochrome ASCII conversion.

utf8_palette_cache_t * get_utf8_palette_cache (const char
  * ascii_chars)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/ascii/common.h>


Get UTF-8 palette cache for a character set.


Definition at line 278 of file
  video/ascii/common.c.


278                                                                       {
279   if (!ascii_chars)
280     return NULL;
281 
282   // Check for empty string
283   if (ascii_chars[0] == '\0')
284     return NULL;
285 
286   // Create hash of palette for cache key
287   uint32_t palette_hash = hash_palette_string(ascii_chars);
288 
289   // Ensure the cache system is initialized
290   init_utf8_cache_system();
291 
292   // First try: read lock for cache lookup (allows multiple concurrent readers)
293   rwlock_rdlock(&g_utf8_cache_rwlock);
294 
295   // Check if cache exists
296   utf8_palette_cache_t *cache = NULL;
297   HASH_FIND_INT(g_utf8_cache_table, &palette_hash, cache);
298   if (cache) {
299     // Cache hit: Update access tracking (atomics are thread-safe under rdlock)
300     uint64_t current_time = time_get_ns();
301     atomic_store_u64(&cache->last_access_time, current_time);
302     uint32_t new_access_count = atomic_fetch_add_u64(&cache->access_count, 1) + 1;
303 
304     // Every 10th access: Update heap position (requires write lock)
305     if (new_access_count % 10 == 0) {
306       // Save the key before releasing read lock
307       uint32_t saved_key = cache->key;
308 
309       // Release read lock and upgrade to write lock
310       rwlock_rdunlock(&g_utf8_cache_rwlock);
311       rwlock_wrlock(&g_utf8_cache_rwlock);
312 
313       // Re-lookup cache entry after lock upgrade
314       // Another thread could have evicted this entry while we were upgrading locks
315       utf8_palette_cache_t *cache_relookup = NULL;
316       HASH_FIND_INT(g_utf8_cache_table, &saved_key, cache_relookup);
317       if (cache_relookup) {
318         // Cache entry still exists - safe to update heap position
319         uint64_t last_access = atomic_load_u64(&cache_relookup->last_access_time);
320         uint32_t access_count = atomic_load_u64(&cache_relookup->access_count);
321         double new_score =
322             calculate_cache_eviction_score(last_access, access_count, cache_relookup->creation_time, current_time);
323         utf8_heap_update_score(cache_relookup, new_score);
324       }
325       // If cache_relookup is NULL, entry was evicted - skip update (not an error)
326 
327       rwlock_wrunlock(&g_utf8_cache_rwlock);
328     } else {
329       rwlock_rdunlock(&g_utf8_cache_rwlock);
330     }
331 
332     return cache;
333   }
334 
335   // Cache miss: Need to create new entry
336   // Release read lock and acquire write lock
337   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_utf8_cache_rwlock);
338   rwlock_wrlock(&g_utf8_cache_rwlock);
339 
340   // Double-check: another thread might have created it while we upgraded locks
341   cache = NULL;
342   HASH_FIND_INT(g_utf8_cache_table, &palette_hash, cache);
343   if (cache) {
344     // Found it! Just update access tracking and return
345     uint64_t current_time = time_get_ns();
346     atomic_store_u64(&cache->last_access_time, current_time);
347     atomic_fetch_add_u64(&cache->access_count, 1);
348     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_utf8_cache_rwlock);
349     return cache;
350   }
351 
352   // Create new cache entry (holding write lock)
353   cache = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(utf8_palette_cache_t), utf8_palette_cache_t *);
354   if (!cache) {
355     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_utf8_cache_rwlock);
356     return NULL;
357   }
358   memset(cache, 0, sizeof(utf8_palette_cache_t));
359 
360   // Set the key for uthash
361   cache->key = palette_hash;
362 
363   // Build both cache types
364   build_utf8_luminance_cache(ascii_chars, cache->cache);
365   build_utf8_ramp64_cache(ascii_chars, cache->cache64, cache->char_index_ramp);
366 
367   // Store palette hash for validation
368   SAFE_STRNCPY(cache->palette_hash, ascii_chars, sizeof(cache->palette_hash));
369   cache->is_valid = true;
370 
371   // Initialize eviction tracking
372   uint64_t current_time = time_get_ns();
373   atomic_store_u64(&cache->last_access_time, current_time);
374   atomic_store_u64(&cache->access_count, 1); // First access
375   cache->creation_time = current_time;
376 
377   // Store in hash table with guaranteed eviction support
378   try_insert_with_eviction_utf8(palette_hash, cache);
379 
380   log_debug("UTF8_CACHE: Created new cache for palette='%s' (hash=0x%x)", ascii_chars, palette_hash);
381 
382   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_utf8_cache_rwlock);
383   return cache;
384 }

void
  image_clear (image_t * p)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/image.h>


Clear image (set all pixels to black).


Parameters


p Image to clear (must not be NULL)


Sets all pixels in the image to black (RGB = 0, 0, 0). Useful for
    resetting image state before filling with new data.




Note


Image dimensions are not modified (only pixel data is
  cleared).



This is equivalent to memset() of pixels array to zero.




Definition at line 205 of file
  video/rgba/image.c.


205                              {
206   if (!p || !p->pixels) {
207     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image_clear: p or p->pixels is NULL");
208     return;
209   }
210   // Check for integer overflow before multiplication.
211   unsigned long w_ul = (unsigned long)p->w;
212   unsigned long h_ul = (unsigned long)p->h;
213   if (w_ul > 0 && h_ul > ULONG_MAX / w_ul) {
214     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image_clear: dimensions overflow: %d x %d", p->w, p->h);
215     return;
216   }
217   unsigned long pixel_count = w_ul * h_ul;
218   if (pixel_count > ULONG_MAX / sizeof(rgb_pixel_t)) {
219     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image_clear: buffer size overflow");
220     return;
221   }
222   size_t clear_size = pixel_count * sizeof(rgb_pixel_t);
223   SAFE_MEMSET(p->pixels, clear_size, 0, clear_size);
224 }

    image_t::pixels, SAFE_MEMSET, SET_ERRNO, and
    image_t::w.

void
  image_destroy (image_t * p)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/image.h>


Destroy an image allocated with image_new().


Parameters


p Image to destroy (can be NULL, no-op if
  NULL)


Frees an image structure and its associated pixel data array
    allocated by image_new(). Frees both the image structure and the
    pixels array.




Note


Safe to call with NULL pointer (no-op).



Must NOT be used for images allocated with
    image_new_from_pool().




Use image_destroy_to_pool() for pool-allocated images.




Definition at line 87 of file
  video/rgba/image.c.


87                                {
88   if (!p) {
89     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image_destroy: p is NULL");
90     return;
91   }
92 
93   // Check allocation method and deallocate appropriately
94   if (p->alloc_method == IMAGE_ALLOC_POOL) {
95     // Pool-allocated images: free entire contiguous buffer (image + pixels)
96     // Validate dimensions before calculating size (guard against corruption)
97     if (p->w <= 0 || p->h <= 0) {
98       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image_destroy: invalid dimensions %dx%d (pool-allocated)", p->w, p->h);
99       return;
100     }
101 
102     // Calculate original allocation size for proper buffer pool free
103     size_t w = (size_t)p->w;
104     size_t h = (size_t)p->h;
105     size_t pixels_size;
106     if (checked_size_mul3(w, h, sizeof(rgb_pixel_t), &pixels_size) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
107       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "image_destroy: dimensions would overflow: %dx%d", p->w, p->h);
108       return;
109     }
110     size_t total_size;
111     if (checked_size_add(sizeof(image_t), pixels_size, &total_size) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
112       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "image_destroy: total size would overflow: %dx%d", p->w, p->h);
113       return;
114     }
115 
116     // Free the entire contiguous buffer back to pool
117     buffer_pool_free(NULL, p, total_size);
118   } else {
119     // SIMD-allocated images: free pixels and structure separately
120     // SAFE_MALLOC_SIMD allocates with aligned allocation (posix_memalign on macOS, aligned_alloc on Linux)
121     // These can be freed with standard free() / SAFE_FREE()
122     SAFE_FREE(p->pixels);
123     SAFE_FREE(p);
124   }
125 }

Returns an image structure and its pixel data to the buffer pool
    for reuse. This is faster than standard free() because buffers are reused
    instead of being deallocated.




Note


Safe to call with NULL pointer (no-op).



Must NOT be used for images allocated with image_new().




Use image_destroy() for standard-allocated images.

image_t * image_new (size_t width, size_t height)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/image.h>


Create a new image with standard allocation.


Parameters


width Image width in pixels (must be > 0 and
  <= IMAGE_MAX_WIDTH)


height Image height in pixels (must be > 0 and <=
  IMAGE_MAX_HEIGHT)


Returns


Allocated image structure, or NULL on error


Allocates a new image structure and pixel data array using
    standard malloc() allocation. Image is initialized with specified dimensions
    and all pixels set to black (0, 0, 0).




Note


Caller must free with image_destroy() when done.



Returns NULL on error (invalid dimensions, memory allocation
    failure).




Pixel data is allocated separately and freed automatically on
    destroy.




Example








image_t *img = image_new(1920, 1080);
if (img) {


    // Use image...


    image_destroy(img);
}

image_t * image_new_copy (const image_t *
  source)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/image.h>


Create a copy of an existing image.


Parameters


source Source image to copy (must not be
  NULL)


Returns


Newly allocated copy of the image, or NULL on error


Creates a deep copy of an image, allocating new memory for the
    copy. The source and destination images are completely independent -
    modifying one does not affect the other. Useful for creating snapshots to
    prevent concurrent modification issues.




Note


Returns NULL on error (invalid image, memory allocation
  failure).



Caller must free the returned image with
  image_destroy().




Copy is allocated with standard malloc() allocation.




Pixels are fully copied (not shared with source).




Definition at line 226 of file
  video/rgba/image.c.


226                                                {
227   if (!source) {
228     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image_new_copy: source is NULL");
229     return NULL;
230   }
231 
232   // Create new image with same dimensions
233   image_t *copy = image_new((size_t)source->w, (size_t)source->h);
234   if (!copy) {
235     return NULL;
236   }
237 
238   // Copy pixel data from source to copy
239   if (source->pixels && copy->pixels) {
240     size_t pixel_count = (size_t)source->w * (size_t)source->h;
241     size_t pixels_size = pixel_count * sizeof(rgb_pixel_t);
242     memcpy(copy->pixels, source->pixels, pixels_size);
243   }
244 
245   return copy;
246 }

image_t * image_new_from_pool (size_t width, size_t
  height)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/image.h>


Create a new image from buffer pool.


Parameters


width Image width in pixels (must be > 0 and
  <= IMAGE_MAX_WIDTH)


height Image height in pixels (must be > 0 and <=
  IMAGE_MAX_HEIGHT)


Returns


Allocated image structure from pool, or NULL on
  error


Allocates a new image structure and pixel data from the buffer
    pool for efficient memory management in video pipeline. Buffer pool reduces
    allocation overhead and improves performance for high-frequency operations
    (video frame capture/processing).




Note


Caller must free with image_destroy_to_pool() when
  done.



Returns NULL on error (invalid dimensions, pool exhaustion).




Buffer pool allocation is faster than standard malloc().




Example








image_t *img = image_new_from_pool(1920, 1080);
if (img) {


    // Process video frame...


    image_destroy_to_pool(img);
}





Definition at line 128 of file
  video/rgba/image.c.

131     return NULL;
132   }
133 
134   if (width > IMAGE_MAX_WIDTH || height > IMAGE_MAX_HEIGHT) {
135     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image_new_from_pool: dimensions %zux%zu exceed maximum %ux%u", width, height,
136               IMAGE_MAX_WIDTH, IMAGE_MAX_HEIGHT);
137     return NULL;
138   }
139 
140   // Calculate total allocation size (structure + pixel data in single buffer)
141   // Check for integer overflow before multiplication
142   size_t pixels_size;

144     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image_new_from_pool: dimensions would overflow: %zux%zu", width, height);
145     return NULL;
146   }
147 
148   size_t total_size;
149   if (checked_size_add(sizeof(image_t), pixels_size, &total_size) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
150     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image_new_from_pool: total size would overflow");
151     return NULL;

153

158               width, height);

161

163   image_t *image = (image_t *)buffer;

165   image->h = (int)height;
166   // Pixel data immediately follows the image structure
167   image->pixels = (rgb_pixel_t *)((uint8_t *)buffer + sizeof(image_t));

169 
170   return image;

char
  * image_print (const image_t * p, const char * palette)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/image.h>


Print image as ASCII art (monochrome).


Parameters


p Image to print (must not be NULL)


Palette Character palette to use (or NULL for default
  ASCII palette)


Returns


Allocated ASCII string (caller must free), or NULL on
  error


Converts an image to ASCII art without color output. Uses
    character palette to map pixel luminance to ASCII characters. Returns a
    null-terminated string containing the ASCII art frame.




Note


Returns NULL on error (invalid image, memory allocation
  failure).



ASCII string must be freed by caller using free().




Character palette is used to map luminance to characters.




ASCII output has width*height characters (one character per
    pixel).

27                                                          {
28   if (!p || !palette) {
29     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image_print: p=%p or palette=%p is NULL", p, palette);
30     return NULL;
31   }
32   if (!p->pixels) {
33     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image_print: p->pixels is NULL");
34     return NULL;
35   }
36 
37   const int h = p->h;
38   const int w = p->w;
39 
40   if (h <= 0 || w <= 0) {
41     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image_print: invalid dimensions h=%d, w=%d", h, w);
42     return NULL;
43   }
44 
45   // Get UTF-8 character cache for proper multi-byte character support
46   utf8_palette_cache_t *utf8_cache = get_utf8_palette_cache(palette);
47   if (!utf8_cache) {
48     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to get UTF-8 palette cache for scalar rendering");
49     return NULL;
50   }
51 
52   // Character index ramp is now part of UTF-8 cache - no separate cache needed
53 
54   // Need space for h rows with UTF-8 characters, plus h-1 newlines, plus null terminator
55   const size_t max_char_bytes = 4; // Max UTF-8 character size
56 
57   const rgb_pixel_t *pix = p->pixels;
58 
59   // Use outbuf_t for efficient UTF-8 RLE emission (same as SIMD renderers)
60   outbuf_t ob = {0};
61 
62   // Calculate buffer size with overflow checking
63   size_t w_times_bytes;
64   if (checked_size_mul((size_t)w, max_char_bytes, &w_times_bytes) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
65     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Buffer size overflow: width too large for UTF-8 encoding");
66     return NULL;
67   }
68 
69   size_t w_times_bytes_plus_one;
70   if (checked_size_add(w_times_bytes, 1, &w_times_bytes_plus_one) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
71     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Buffer size overflow: width * bytes + 1 overflow");
72     return NULL;
73   }
74 
75   if (checked_size_mul((size_t)h, w_times_bytes_plus_one, &ob.cap) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
76     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Buffer size overflow: height * (width * bytes + 1) overflow");
77     return NULL;
78   }
79 
80   ob.buf = SAFE_MALLOC(ob.cap ? ob.cap : 1, char *);
81   if (!ob.buf) {
82     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate output buffer for scalar rendering");
83     return NULL;
84   }
85 
86   // Process pixels with UTF-8 RLE emission (same approach as SIMD)
87   for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
88     const int row_offset = y * w;
89 
90     for (int x = 0; x < w;) {
91       const rgb_pixel_t pixel = pix[row_offset + x];
92       // Use same luminance formula as SIMD: ITU-R BT.601 with rounding
93       const int luminance = (77 * pixel.r + 150 * pixel.g + 29 * pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
94 
95       // Use same 6-bit precision as SIMD: map luminance (0-255) to bucket (0-63) then to character
96       uint8_t safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(luminance);
97       uint8_t luma_idx = (uint8_t)(safe_luminance >> 2);        // 0-63 index (same as SIMD)
98       uint8_t char_idx = utf8_cache->char_index_ramp[luma_idx]; // Map to character index (same as SIMD)
99 
100       // Use same 64-entry cache as SIMD for consistency
101       const utf8_char_t *char_info = &utf8_cache->cache64[luma_idx];
102 
103       // Find run length for same character (RLE optimization)
104       int j = x + 1;
105       while (j < w) {
106         const rgb_pixel_t next_pixel = pix[row_offset + j];
107         const int next_luminance = (77 * next_pixel.r + 150 * next_pixel.g + 29 * next_pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
108         uint8_t next_safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(next_luminance);
109         uint8_t next_luma_idx = (uint8_t)(next_safe_luminance >> 2);        // 0-63 index (same as SIMD)
110         uint8_t next_char_idx = utf8_cache->char_index_ramp[next_luma_idx]; // Map to character index (same as SIMD)
111         if (next_char_idx != char_idx)
112           break;
113         j++;
114       }
115       uint32_t run = (uint32_t)(j - x);
116 
117       // Emit UTF-8 character with RLE (same as SIMD)
118       ob_write(&ob, (const char *)char_info->utf8_bytes, char_info->byte_len);
119       if (rep_is_profitable(run)) {
120         emit_rep(&ob, run - 1);
121       } else {
122         for (uint32_t k = 1; k < run; k++) {
123           ob_write(&ob, (const char *)char_info->utf8_bytes, char_info->byte_len);
124         }
125       }
126       x = j;
127     }
128 
129     // Add newline between rows (except last row)
130     if (y != h - 1) {
131       ob_putc(&ob, '\n');
132     }
133   }
134 
135   ob_term(&ob);
136   return ob.buf;
137 }

  * image_print_16color (const image_t * image, const char *
  palette)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/image.h>


Print image using 16-color ANSI mode.


Parameters


image Image to print (must not be NULL)


Palette Character palette to use (or NULL for default
  ASCII palette)


Returns


Allocated ASCII string with 16-color ANSI codes (caller
  must free), or NULL on error


Converts an image to ASCII art using 16-color ANSI mode. Uses
    standard ANSI color palette (0-15) for color output. Colors are quantized to
    16-color palette.




Note


Returns NULL on error (invalid image, memory allocation
  failure).



ASCII string must be freed by caller using free().




Requires terminal 16-color support for proper display.




Colors are quantized to 16-color palette automatically.

456     return NULL;
457   }
458 
459   int h = image->h;
460   int w = image->w;
461 
462   if (h <= 0 || w <= 0) {
463     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "image_print_16color: invalid dimensions h=%d, w=%d", h, w);
464     return NULL;
465   }
466 
467   // Initialize 16-color lookup table
468   ansi_fast_init_16color();
469 
470   // Calculate buffer size (smaller than 256-color due to shorter ANSI sequences)
471   // Space for ANSI codes + newlines
472   // Calculate with overflow checking
473   size_t h_times_w;
474   if (checked_size_mul((size_t)h, (size_t)w, &h_times_w) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
475     return NULL;
476   }
477 
478   size_t h_times_w_times_12;
479   if (checked_size_mul(h_times_w, 12u, &h_times_w_times_12) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
480     return NULL;
481   }
482 
483   size_t buffer_size;
484   if (checked_size_add(h_times_w_times_12, (size_t)h, &buffer_size) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
485     return NULL;
486   }
487 
488   char *buffer;
489   buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(buffer_size, char *);
490 
491   char *ptr = buffer;
492   const char *reset_code = "\033[0m";
493 
494   for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
495     for (int x = 0; x < w; x++) {
496       rgb_pixel_t pixel = image->pixels[y * w + x];
497 
498       // Convert RGB to 16-color index and generate ANSI sequence
499       uint8_t color_index = rgb_to_16color(pixel.r, pixel.g, pixel.b);
500       ptr = append_16color_fg(ptr, color_index);
501 
502       // Use same luminance formula as SIMD: ITU-R BT.601 with rounding
503       int luminance = (77 * pixel.r + 150 * pixel.g + 29 * pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
504 
505       // Use UTF-8 cache which contains character index ramp
506       utf8_palette_cache_t *utf8_cache = get_utf8_palette_cache(palette);
507       if (!utf8_cache) {
508         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to get UTF-8 cache");
509         return NULL;
510       }
511 
512       // Use same 6-bit precision as SIMD: map luminance (0-255) to bucket (0-63) then to character
513       uint8_t safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(luminance);
514       uint8_t luma_idx = (uint8_t)(safe_luminance >> 2);        // 0-63 index (same as SIMD)
515       uint8_t char_idx = utf8_cache->char_index_ramp[luma_idx]; // Map to character index (same as SIMD)
516 
517       // Use direct palette character lookup (same as SIMD would do)
518       char ascii_char = palette[char_idx];
519       const utf8_char_t *char_info = &utf8_cache->cache[(unsigned char)ascii_char];
520 
521       if (char_info) {
522         // Copy UTF-8 character bytes
523         for (int byte_idx = 0; byte_idx < char_info->byte_len; byte_idx++) {
524           *ptr++ = char_info->utf8_bytes[byte_idx];
525         }
526       } else {
527         // Fallback to simple ASCII if UTF-8 cache fails
528         size_t palette_len = strlen(palette);
529         int palette_index = (luminance * ((int)palette_len - 1) + 127) / 255;
530         *ptr++ = palette[palette_index];
531       }
532     }
533 
534     // Add reset and newline at end of each row
535     SAFE_STRNCPY(ptr, reset_code, 5);
536     ptr += strlen(reset_code);
537     if (y < h - 1) {
538       *ptr++ = '\n';
539     }
540   }
541 
542   *ptr = '\0';
543   return buffer;
544 }

char
  * image_print_16color_dithered (const image_t * image, const char *
  palette)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/image.h>


Print image using 16-color ANSI mode with dithering.


Parameters


image Image to print (must not be NULL)


Palette Character palette to use (or NULL for default
  ASCII palette)


Returns


Allocated ASCII string with dithered 16-color ANSI codes
  (caller must free), or NULL on error


Converts an image to ASCII art using 16-color ANSI mode with
    Floyd-Steinberg dithering. Dithering improves color accuracy by distributing
    quantization errors across neighboring pixels.




DITHERING:


    
  
  • Floyd-Steinberg error diffusion algorithm
    • 
  
  • Distributes quantization errors to adjacent pixels
    • 
  
  • Improves perceived color accuracy in 16-color mode
    • 
  
  • Slight performance overhead compared to non-dithered mode
    • 



Note


Returns NULL on error (invalid image, memory allocation
  failure).



ASCII string must be freed by caller using free().




Dithering improves color accuracy but may add noise.




Requires terminal 16-color support for proper display.

571   if (!image || !image->pixels || !palette) {

573     return NULL;
574   }
575 
576   int h = image->h;
577   int w = image->w;
578 
579   if (h <= 0 || w <= 0) {
580     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "image_print_16color_dithered: invalid dimensions h=%d, w=%d", h, w);
581     return NULL;
582   }
583 
584   // Initialize 16-color lookup table
585   ansi_fast_init_16color();
586

592   // Calculate buffer size (same as non-dithered version)
593   // Space for ANSI codes + newlines
594   // Calculate with overflow checking
595   size_t h_times_w2;

597     SAFE_FREE(error_buffer);

599   }
600 
601   size_t h_times_w2_times_12;

603     SAFE_FREE(error_buffer);
604     return NULL;
605   }
606 
607   size_t buffer_size;
608   if (checked_size_add(h_times_w2_times_12, (size_t)h, &buffer_size) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
609     SAFE_FREE(error_buffer);
610     return NULL;
611   }
612 
613   char *buffer;
614   buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(buffer_size, char *);
615 
616   char *ptr = buffer;
617   const char *reset_code = "\033[0m";
618 
619   for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
620     for (int x = 0; x < w; x++) {
621       rgb_pixel_t pixel = image->pixels[y * w + x];
622 
623       // Convert RGB to 16-color index using dithering
624       uint8_t color_index = rgb_to_16color_dithered(pixel.r, pixel.g, pixel.b, x, y, w, h, error_buffer);
625       ptr = append_16color_fg(ptr, color_index);
626 
627       // Use same luminance formula as SIMD: ITU-R BT.601 with rounding
628       int luminance = (77 * pixel.r + 150 * pixel.g + 29 * pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
629 
630       // Use UTF-8 cache which contains character index ramp
631       utf8_palette_cache_t *utf8_cache = get_utf8_palette_cache(palette);
632       if (!utf8_cache) {
633         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to get UTF-8 cache");
634         return NULL;
635       }
636 
637       // Use same 6-bit precision as SIMD: map luminance (0-255) to bucket (0-63) then to character
638       uint8_t safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(luminance);
639       uint8_t luma_idx = (uint8_t)(safe_luminance >> 2);        // 0-63 index (same as SIMD)
640       uint8_t char_idx = utf8_cache->char_index_ramp[luma_idx]; // Map to character index (same as SIMD)
641 
642       // Use direct palette character lookup (same as SIMD would do)
643       char ascii_char = palette[char_idx];
644       const utf8_char_t *char_info = &utf8_cache->cache[(unsigned char)ascii_char];
645 
646       if (char_info) {
647         // Copy UTF-8 character bytes
648         for (int byte_idx = 0; byte_idx < char_info->byte_len; byte_idx++) {
649           *ptr++ = char_info->utf8_bytes[byte_idx];
650         }
651       } else {
652         // Fallback to simple ASCII if UTF-8 cache fails
653         size_t palette_len = strlen(palette);
654         int palette_index = (luminance * ((int)palette_len - 1) + 127) / 255;
655         *ptr++ = palette[palette_index];
656       }
657     }
658 
659     // Add reset and newline at end of each row
660     SAFE_STRNCPY(ptr, reset_code, 5);
661     ptr += strlen(reset_code);
662     if (y < h - 1) {
663       *ptr++ = '\n';
664     }
665   }
666 
667   *ptr = '\0';
668   SAFE_FREE(error_buffer); // Clean up error buffer
669   return buffer;
670 }






References ansi_fast_init_16color(),
    append_16color_fg(), ASCIICHAT_OK, buffer_size,
    utf8_char_t::byte_len, utf8_palette_cache::cache,
    utf8_palette_cache::char_index_ramp, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_INVALID_STATE, get_utf8_palette_cache(),
    image_t::h, image_t::pixels, rgb_to_16color_dithered(),
    SAFE_CALLOC, SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC,
    SAFE_STRNCPY, SET_ERRNO, utf8_char_t::utf8_bytes, and
    image_t::w.






char
  * image_print_16color_dithered_with_background (const image_t * image,
  bool use_background, const char * palette)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/image.h>


Print image using 16-color ANSI mode with dithering and background
    colors.


Parameters


image Image to print (must not be NULL)


use_background Whether to use background colors (true) or foreground
  colors only (false)


Palette Character palette to use (or NULL for default
  ASCII palette)


Returns


Allocated ASCII string with dithered 16-color ANSI codes
  (caller must free), or NULL on error


Converts an image to ASCII art using 16-color ANSI mode with
    Floyd-Steinberg dithering and optional background color support. Background
    colors provide better color coverage for block-style rendering.




RENDERING MODES:


    
  
  • Foreground colors: Character color is set (text color)
    • 
  
  • Background colors: Character background is set (block color)
    • 
  
  • Background mode provides better color coverage for block characters
    • 



Note


Returns NULL on error (invalid image, memory allocation
  failure).



ASCII string must be freed by caller using free().




Background colors require terminal background color support.




Dithering improves color accuracy but may add noise.




Definition at line 673 of file foreground.c.


673                                                                                                                    {
674   if (!image || !image->pixels || !palette) {
675     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image=%p or image->pixels=%p or palette=%p is NULL", image, image->pixels, palette);
676     return NULL;
677   }
678 
679   int h = image->h;
680   int w = image->w;
681 
682   if (h <= 0 || w <= 0) {
683     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "image_print_16color_dithered_with_background: invalid dimensions h=%d, w=%d", h, w);
684     return NULL;
685   }
686 
687   // Initialize 16-color lookup table
688   ansi_fast_init_16color();
689 
690   // Allocate error buffer for Floyd-Steinberg dithering
691   size_t pixel_count = (size_t)h * (size_t)w;
692   rgb_error_t *error_buffer;
693   error_buffer = SAFE_CALLOC(pixel_count, sizeof(rgb_error_t), rgb_error_t *);
694 
695   // Calculate buffer size (larger for background mode due to more ANSI sequences)
696   size_t buffer_size =
697       (size_t)h * (size_t)w * (use_background ? 24 : 12) + (size_t)h; // Space for ANSI codes + newlines
698   char *buffer;
699   buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(buffer_size, char *);
700 
701   char *ptr = buffer;
702   const char *reset_code = "\033[0m";
703 
704   for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
705     for (int x = 0; x < w; x++) {
706       rgb_pixel_t pixel = image->pixels[y * w + x];
707 
708       // Convert RGB to 16-color index using dithering
709       uint8_t color_index = rgb_to_16color_dithered(pixel.r, pixel.g, pixel.b, x, y, w, h, error_buffer);
710 
711       if (use_background) {
712         // Background mode: use contrasting foreground on background color
713         uint8_t bg_r, bg_g, bg_b;
714         get_16color_rgb(color_index, &bg_r, &bg_g, &bg_b);
715 
716         // Calculate luminance to choose contrasting foreground
717         int bg_luminance = (bg_r * 77 + bg_g * 150 + bg_b * 29) / 256;
718         uint8_t fg_color = (bg_luminance < 127) ? 15 : 0; // White on dark, black on bright
719 
720         ptr = append_16color_bg(ptr, color_index); // Set background to pixel color
721         ptr = append_16color_fg(ptr, fg_color);    // Set contrasting foreground
722       } else {
723         // Foreground mode: set foreground to pixel color
724         ptr = append_16color_fg(ptr, color_index);
725       }
726 
727       // Use same luminance formula as SIMD: ITU-R BT.601 with rounding
728       int luminance = (77 * pixel.r + 150 * pixel.g + 29 * pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
729 
730       // Use UTF-8 cache which contains character index ramp
731       utf8_palette_cache_t *utf8_cache = get_utf8_palette_cache(palette);
732       if (!utf8_cache) {
733         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to get UTF-8 cache");
734         return NULL;
735       }
736 
737       // Use same 6-bit precision as SIMD: map luminance (0-255) to bucket (0-63) then to character
738       uint8_t safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(luminance);
739       uint8_t luma_idx = (uint8_t)(safe_luminance >> 2);        // 0-63 index (same as SIMD)
740       uint8_t char_idx = utf8_cache->char_index_ramp[luma_idx]; // Map to character index (same as SIMD)
741 
742       // Use direct palette character lookup (same as SIMD would do)
743       char ascii_char = palette[char_idx];
744       const utf8_char_t *char_info = &utf8_cache->cache[(unsigned char)ascii_char];
745 
746       if (char_info) {
747         // Copy UTF-8 character bytes
748         for (int byte_idx = 0; byte_idx < char_info->byte_len; byte_idx++) {
749           *ptr++ = char_info->utf8_bytes[byte_idx];
750         }
751       } else {
752         // Fallback to simple ASCII if UTF-8 cache fails
753         size_t palette_len = strlen(palette);
754         int palette_index = (luminance * ((int)palette_len - 1) + 127) / 255;
755         *ptr++ = palette[palette_index];
756       }
757     }
758 
759     // Add reset and newline at end of each row
760     SAFE_STRNCPY(ptr, reset_code, 5);
761     ptr += strlen(reset_code);
762     if (y < h - 1) {
763       *ptr++ = '\n';
764     }
765   }
766 
767   *ptr = '\0';
768   SAFE_FREE(error_buffer); // Clean up error buffer
769   return buffer;
770 }

Palette Character palette to use (or NULL for default
  ASCII palette)


Returns


Allocated ASCII string with 256-color ANSI codes (caller
  must free), or NULL on error


Converts an image to ASCII art using 256-color ANSI mode. Uses
    extended ANSI color palette (0-255) for color output. Colors are quantized
    to 256-color palette.




Note


Returns NULL on error (invalid image, memory allocation
  failure).



ASCII string must be freed by caller using free().




Requires terminal 256-color support for proper display.




Colors are quantized to 256-color palette automatically.

char
  * image_print_color (const image_t * p, const char * palette)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/image.h>


Print image as ASCII art with color.


Parameters


p Image to print (must not be NULL)


Palette Character palette to use (or NULL for default
  ASCII palette)


Returns


Allocated ASCII string with ANSI color codes (caller must
  free), or NULL on error


Converts an image to ASCII art with color output. Uses character
    palette for character mapping and ANSI color codes for color output. Returns
    a null-terminated string containing the ASCII art frame with embedded ANSI
    escape sequences.




Note


Returns NULL on error (invalid image, memory allocation
  failure).



ASCII string must be freed by caller using free().




ANSI color codes are embedded in the output string.




Color output requires terminal color support for proper
  display.




Converts an image to colored ASCII art with ANSI escape codes.




This function generates a string representation of an image where
    each pixel is converted to an ASCII character with ANSI color codes. The
    character is chosen based on luminance, and colors are applied using 24-bit
    RGB ANSI escape sequences.




Buffer allocation is precisely calculated to avoid waste and
    prevent overflows:


    
  
  • Each pixel: 1 ASCII char + foreground ANSI code (19 bytes max)
    • 
  
  • Background mode: adds background ANSI code (19 bytes max per pixel)
    • 
  
  • Each row: reset sequence (\033[0m = 4 bytes) + newline (except last
    row)
    • 
  
  • At the end: null terminator (1 byte)
    • 



Color modes:


    
  
  • Foreground only (default): ASCII characters with colored foreground
    • 
  
  • Background mode (opt_background_color): colored background with
      contrasting foreground (black on bright backgrounds, white on dark
      backgrounds)
    • 



ANSI escape code format:


    
  
  • Foreground: \033[38;2;R;G;Bm (11-19 bytes depending on RGB values)
    • 
  
  • Background: \033[48;2;R;G;Bm (11-19 bytes depending on RGB values)
    • 
  
  • Reset: \033[0m (4 bytes)
    • 



Parameters


p Pointer to image_t structure containing
  pixel data


Returns


Dynamically allocated string containing colored ASCII
  art, or NULL on error. Caller is responsible for freeing the returned
  string.


Note


The function performs overflow checks to prevent integer
  overflow when calculating buffer sizes for very large images.



Uses global opt_background_color to determine color mode.




Exits with ERROR_BUFFER if buffer overflow is detected during
    string construction (should never happen with correct calculation).




Definition at line 191 of file foreground.c.


191                                                                {
192   if (!p || !palette) {
193     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "p=%p or palette=%p is NULL", p, palette);
194     return NULL;
195   }
196   if (!p->pixels) {
197     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "p->pixels is NULL");
198     return NULL;
199   }
200 
201   // Get UTF-8 character cache for proper multi-byte character support
202   utf8_palette_cache_t *utf8_cache = get_utf8_palette_cache(palette);
203   if (!utf8_cache) {
204     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to get UTF-8 palette cache for scalar color rendering");
205     return NULL;
206   }
207 
208   const int h = p->h;
209   const int w = p->w;
210 
211   // Constants for ANSI escape codes (using exact sizes from ansi_fast.c)
212   const size_t max_fg_ansi = 19; // \033[38;2;255;255;255m
213   const size_t max_bg_ansi = 19; // \033[48;2;255;255;255m
214   const size_t reset_len = 4;    // \033[0m
215 
216   const size_t h_sz = (size_t)h;
217   const size_t w_sz = (size_t)w;
218 
219   // Ensure h * w won't overflow
220   if (h_sz > 0 && w_sz > SIZE_MAX / h_sz) {
221     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Image dimensions too large: %d x %d", h, w);
222     return NULL;
223   }
224 
225   const size_t total_pixels = h_sz * w_sz;
226   const size_t bytes_per_pixel = 1 + max_fg_ansi + max_bg_ansi; // Conservative estimate for max possible
227 
228   // Ensure total_pixels * bytes_per_pixel won't overflow
229   if (total_pixels > SIZE_MAX / bytes_per_pixel) {
230     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Pixel data too large for buffer: %d x %d", h, w);
231     return NULL;
232   }
233 
234   const size_t pixel_bytes = total_pixels * bytes_per_pixel;
235 
236   // Per row: newline (except last row)
237   // Final reset added once by ansi_rle_finish()
238   const size_t total_newlines = (h_sz > 0) ? (h_sz - 1) : 0;
239   const size_t final_reset = reset_len; // One reset at end (from ansi_rle_finish)
240 
241   // Final buffer size: pixel bytes + per-row newlines + final reset + null terminator
242   const size_t extra_bytes = total_newlines + final_reset + 1;
243 
244   if (pixel_bytes > SIZE_MAX - extra_bytes) {
245     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Final buffer size would overflow: %d x %d", h, w);
246     return NULL;
247   }
248 
249   const size_t lines_size = pixel_bytes + extra_bytes;
250   char *lines;
251   lines = SAFE_MALLOC(lines_size, char *);
252 
253   const rgb_pixel_t *pix = p->pixels;
254   // char *current_pos = lines;
255   // const char *buffer_end = lines + lines_size - 1; // reserve space for '\0'
256 
257   // Initialize the optimized RLE context for color sequence caching
258   // Note: This function should be called via image_print_with_capabilities() for proper per-client rendering
259   ansi_rle_context_t rle_ctx;
260   ansi_color_mode_t color_mode = ANSI_MODE_FOREGROUND; // Default to foreground-only for legacy usage
261   ansi_rle_init(&rle_ctx, lines, lines_size, color_mode);
262 
263   // Process each pixel using the optimized RLE context
264   for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
265     const int row_offset = y * w;
266 
267     for (int x = 0; x < w; x++) {
268       const rgb_pixel_t pixel = pix[row_offset + x];
269       int r = pixel.r, g = pixel.g, b = pixel.b;
270       // Standard ITU-R BT.601 luminance calculation
271       const int luminance = (77 * r + 150 * g + 29 * b + 128) >> 8;
272 
273       // Use UTF-8 character cache for proper character selection
274       uint8_t safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(luminance);
275       const utf8_char_t *char_info = &utf8_cache->cache[safe_luminance];
276 
277       // For RLE, we need to pass the first byte of the UTF-8 character
278       // Note: RLE system may need updates for full UTF-8 support
279       const char ascii_char = char_info->utf8_bytes[0];
280 
281       ansi_rle_add_pixel(&rle_ctx, (uint8_t)r, (uint8_t)g, (uint8_t)b, ascii_char);
282     }
283 
284     // Add newline after each row (except the last row)
285     if (y != h - 1 && rle_ctx.length < rle_ctx.capacity - 1) {
286       rle_ctx.buffer[rle_ctx.length++] = '\n';
287     }
288   }
289 
290   ansi_rle_finish(&rle_ctx);
291 
292   // DEBUG: Validate frame integrity right after rendering
293   // Check for incomplete ANSI sequences line by line
294   {
295     const char *line_start = lines;
296     int line_num = 0;
297     while (*line_start) {
298       const char *line_end = line_start;
299       while (*line_end && *line_end != '\n')
300         line_end++;
301 
302       // Check for incomplete ANSI sequence at end of this line
303       for (const char *p = line_start; p < line_end;) {
304         if (*p == '\033' && p + 1 < line_end && *(p + 1) == '[') {
305           const char *seq_start = p;
306           p += 2;
307           // Scan for terminator (@ through ~, i.e., 0x40-0x7E)
308           while (p < line_end && !(*p >= '@' && *p <= '~'))
309             p++;
310           if (p >= line_end) {
311             // Incomplete sequence found
312             size_t incomplete_len = (size_t)(line_end - seq_start);
313             log_error("RENDER_INCOMPLETE_ANSI: Line %d (w=%d h=%d) has incomplete ANSI sequence (%zu bytes)", line_num,
314                       w, h, incomplete_len);
315             // Show the incomplete sequence
316             char debug_buf[128] = {0};
317             size_t debug_pos = 0;
318             for (const char *d = seq_start; d < line_end && debug_pos < sizeof(debug_buf) - 10; d++) {
319               unsigned char c = (unsigned char)*d;
320               if (c == '\033') {
321                 debug_buf[debug_pos++] = '<';
322                 debug_buf[debug_pos++] = 'E';
323                 debug_buf[debug_pos++] = 'S';
324                 debug_buf[debug_pos++] = 'C';
325                 debug_buf[debug_pos++] = '>';
326               } else if (c >= 0x20 && c < 0x7F) {
327                 debug_buf[debug_pos++] = (char)c;
328               } else {
329                 debug_pos += (size_t)safe_snprintf(debug_buf + debug_pos, sizeof(debug_buf) - debug_pos, "<%02X>", c);
330               }
331             }
332             log_error("  Incomplete seq: %s", debug_buf);
333             break;
334           } else {
335             p++; // Skip terminator
336           }
337         } else {
338           p++;
339         }
340       }
341 
342       line_num++;
343       line_start = *line_end ? line_end + 1 : line_end;
344     }
345   }
346 
347   return lines;
348 }

char
  * image_print_with_capabilities (const image_t * image, const
  terminal_capabilities_t * caps, const char * palette)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/ascii/ascii.h>


Convert image to ASCII art with terminal capabilities. Print image
    with terminal capability awareness.




Parameters


image Image to convert (must not be NULL)


caps Terminal capabilities structure


Palette Palette characters to use for
  conversion


Returns


ASCII art string, or NULL on error


Converts an image to ASCII art using the provided terminal
    capabilities. Automatically selects the best rendering method based on
    terminal capabilities.




Note


Returns dynamically allocated string that must be freed
  by caller.


Parameters


image Image to print (must not be NULL)


caps Terminal capabilities structure (must not be NULL)


Palette Character palette to use (or NULL for default
  ASCII palette)


luminance_palette Luminance-to-character mapping palette (must not be
  NULL, 256 elements)


Returns


Allocated ASCII string (caller must free), or NULL on
  error


Converts an image to ASCII art with automatic color mode selection
    based on terminal capabilities. Automatically chooses the best color mode
    (16-color, 256-color, or truecolor) for the terminal.




COLOR MODE SELECTION:


    
  
  • Truecolor: If terminal supports 24-bit RGB colors
    • 
  
  • 256-color: If terminal supports extended ANSI palette
    • 
  
  • 16-color: Fallback to standard ANSI colors
    • 
  
  • Monochrome: If terminal has no color support
    • 



Note


Returns NULL on error (invalid image, memory allocation
  failure).



ASCII string must be freed by caller using free().




Color mode is selected automatically based on terminal
    capabilities.




This is the recommended function for capability-aware ASCII
    conversion.




Print image with terminal capability awareness.




Parameters


image Image to convert (must not be NULL)


caps Terminal capabilities structure


Palette Palette characters to use for
  conversion


Returns


ASCII art string, or NULL on error


Converts an image to ASCII art using the provided terminal
    capabilities. Automatically selects the best rendering method based on
    terminal capabilities.




Note


Returns dynamically allocated string that must be freed
  by caller.


Parameters


image Image to print (must not be NULL)


caps Terminal capabilities structure (must not be NULL)


Palette Character palette to use (or NULL for default
  ASCII palette)


luminance_palette Luminance-to-character mapping palette (must not be

Allocated ASCII string (caller must free), or NULL on

Converts an image to ASCII art with automatic color mode selection

    (16-color, 256-color, or truecolor) for the terminal.




COLOR MODE SELECTION:


    
  
  • Truecolor: If terminal supports 24-bit RGB colors
    • 
  
  • 256-color: If terminal supports extended ANSI palette
    • 
  
  • 16-color: Fallback to standard ANSI colors
    • 
  
  • Monochrome: If terminal has no color support
    • 



Note


Returns NULL on error (invalid image, memory allocation
  failure).



ASCII string must be freed by caller using free().




Color mode is selected automatically based on terminal
    capabilities.




This is the recommended function for capability-aware ASCII
    conversion.

Resize image using nearest-neighbor interpolation.

Resizes an image using nearest-neighbor interpolation. Fast but
    may produce aliasing artifacts. Source pixels are sampled to nearest
    destination pixel positions.

  • Fast algorithm (no interpolation calculations)
    • 
  
  • May produce aliasing artifacts
    • 
  
  • Pixel values are copied directly (no averaging)
    • 
  
  • Suitable for integer scaling (2x, 3x, etc.)

Source and destination images must be valid (not NULL).




Fast algorithm suitable for real-time resizing.

  dest)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/image.h>


Resize image using bilinear interpolation.


Parameters


source Source image (must not be NULL)


dest Destination image (must be allocated and have valid
  dimensions)


Resizes an image using bilinear interpolation. Produces smoother
    results than nearest-neighbor but slower. Pixel values are interpolated from
    four nearest source pixels.




BILINEAR INTERPOLATION:


    
  
  • Slower algorithm (interpolation calculations required)
    • 
  
  • Produces smoother results (reduced aliasing)
    • 
  
  • Pixel values are interpolated from 4 nearest neighbors
    • 
  
  • Suitable for fractional scaling (1.5x, 2.3x, etc.)

Note


Destination image dimensions must be set before calling.



Source and destination images must be valid (not NULL).




Slower algorithm but produces better quality.

#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/ascii/output_buffer.h>


Reserve buffer space for upcoming writes.


Parameters


ob Output buffer structure (must not be NULL)


need Minimum bytes needed (must be > 0)


Ensures the buffer has at least 'need' bytes of available space.
    Automatically expands the buffer if necessary. Buffer grows exponentially
    (doubles in size) to minimize reallocation overhead.




Note


Buffer is automatically allocated on first reservation.

This function does not modify buffer length (only capacity).

26     // Always allocate at least default capacity on first call
27     size_t ncap = 4096;
28     while (ncap < ob->len + need)
29       ncap = (ncap * 3) / 2;
30     // SAFE_REALLOC handles NULL ptr as malloc - this is safe
31     ob->buf = SAFE_REALLOC(ob->buf, ncap, char *);
32     ob->cap = ncap;
33   } else if (ob->len + need > ob->cap) {
34     // Expand existing buffer
35     size_t ncap = ob->cap;
36     while (ncap < ob->len + need)
37       ncap = (ncap * 3) / 2;

#include

Precalculate RGB palettes with color adjustment.

  <1.0 = decrease)


blue Blue adjustment factor (1.0 = no adjustment, >1.0 = increase,
  <1.0 = decrease)


Precalculates RGB color palettes with color adjustment factors.
    Color adjustment allows brightness, contrast, and color balance adjustments
    to be applied efficiently during ASCII conversion.




COLOR ADJUSTMENT:


    
  
  • Adjustment factors are multiplied with color components
    • 
  
  • Factors > 1.0 increase color intensity
    • 
  
  • Factors < 1.0 decrease color intensity
    • 
  
  • Precalculated palettes improve conversion performance
    • 



Note


Precalculated palettes are cached for efficient
  conversion.



Color adjustment is applied during ASCII conversion.




Useful for brightness/contrast control.

  video/rgba/image.c.

333   // Validate input parameters to prevent overflow
334   // Luminance weights should typically be in range 0.0-1.0
335   // But allow slightly larger values for brightness adjustment
336   const float max_weight = 255.0f;  // Maximum value that won't overflow when multiplied by 255
337   const float min_weight = -255.0f; // Allow negative for color correction
338 
339   // Note: isfinite() checks are skipped in Release builds due to -ffinite-math-only flag
340   // which disables NaN/infinity support for performance. Range checks below are sufficient.
341 #if !defined(NDEBUG) && !defined(__FAST_MATH__)
342   if (!isfinite(red) || !isfinite(green) || !isfinite(blue)) {
343     log_error("Invalid weight values (non-finite): red=%f, green=%f, blue=%f", red, green, blue);
344     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "precalc_rgb_palettes: non-finite weight values");
345     return;
346   }
347 #endif
348 
349   if (red < min_weight || red > max_weight || green < min_weight || green > max_weight || blue < min_weight ||
350       blue > max_weight) {
351     log_warn(
352         "precalc_rgb_palettes: Weight values out of expected range: red=%f, green=%f, blue=%f (clamping to safe range)",
353         red, green, blue);
354   }
355 
356   // Clamp weights to safe range to prevent overflow
357   const float safe_red = (red < min_weight) ? min_weight : ((red > max_weight) ? max_weight : red);
358   const float safe_green = (green < min_weight) ? min_weight : ((green > max_weight) ? max_weight : green);
359   const float safe_blue = (blue < min_weight) ? min_weight : ((blue > max_weight) ? max_weight : blue);
360 
361   const unsigned short max_ushort = 65535;
362   const unsigned short min_ushort = 0;
363 
364   for (int n = 0; n < ASCII_LUMINANCE_LEVELS; ++n) {
365     // Compute with float, then clamp to unsigned short range
366     float red_val = (float)n * safe_red;
367     float green_val = (float)n * safe_green;
368     float blue_val = (float)n * safe_blue;
369 
370     // Clamp to unsigned short range before assignment
371     if (red_val < (float)min_ushort) {
372       red_val = (float)min_ushort;
373     } else if (red_val > (float)max_ushort) {
374       red_val = (float)max_ushort;
375     }
376 
377     if (green_val < (float)min_ushort) {
378       green_val = (float)min_ushort;
379     } else if (green_val > (float)max_ushort) {
380       green_val = (float)max_ushort;
381     }
382 
383     if (blue_val < (float)min_ushort) {
384       blue_val = (float)min_ushort;
385     } else if (blue_val > (float)max_ushort) {
386       blue_val = (float)max_ushort;
387     }
388

390     GREEN[n] = (unsigned short)green_val;
391     BLUE[n] = (unsigned short)blue_val;
392     GRAY[n] = (unsigned short)n;

void
  quantize_color (int * r, int * g, int * b, int levels)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/image.h>


Quantize color to specified number of levels.


Parameters


r Red component (input/output, must not be NULL,
  range 0-255)


g Green component (input/output, must not be NULL, range 0-255)


b Blue component (input/output, must not be NULL, range
  0-255)


levels Number of quantization levels (must be > 0)


Quantizes RGB color components to a specified number of levels.
    Reduces color precision for color palette mapping. Input colors are modified
    in-place to quantized values.




QUANTIZATION:


    
  
  • Divides color range (0-255) into 'levels' bins
    • 
  
  • Maps each component to nearest bin center
    • 
  
  • Reduces color precision for palette mapping
    • 
  
  • Used for 16-color and 256-color mode conversion
    • 



Note


Input/output parameters are modified in-place.



Quantization reduces color accuracy but enables palette
  mapping.




Typical values: 2 levels for 16-color, 6 levels for 256-color.




Definition at line 140 of file foreground.c.


140                                                         {
141   if (!r || !g || !b) {
142     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "quantize_color: r, g, or b is NULL");
143     return;
144   }
145 
146   if (levels <= 0) {
147     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "quantize_color: levels must be positive, got %d", levels);
148     return;
149   }
150 
151   int step = 256 / levels;
152   *r = (*r / step) * step;
153   *g = (*g / step) * step;
154   *b = (*b / step) * step;
155 }

char
  * rainbow_replace_ansi_colors (const char * ansi_string, float
  time_seconds)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/color_filter.h>


Replace all RGB ANSI codes with rainbow color.


Parameters


ansi_string Input string with ANSI escape
  sequences


time_seconds Current time in seconds (for rainbow animation)


Returns


New string with rainbow colors, or NULL if no changes
  needed


Replaces all RGB foreground color codes (\x1b[38;2;R;G;Bm) in the
    input string with a single rainbow color calculated from the current time.
    This preserves ASCII character selection while applying rainbow colors.




The function:



  
1.

  
Calculates rainbow color from time using
      color_filter_calculate_rainbow()

  
2.

  
Builds replacement ANSI code string

  
3.

  
Scans input for \x1b[38;2; sequences

  
4.

  
Replaces each RGB code with rainbow code

  
5.

  
Returns new string (caller must SAFE_FREE)




Note


Returns NULL if no ANSI codes found (use original string)



Caller must free returned string with SAFE_FREE()




Input string is not modified




Usable in both terminal apps (lib/session) and WASM apps
  (src/web)




Definition at line 348 of file color_filter.c.

349   if (!ansi_string) {
350     return NULL;
351   }
352 
353   // Calculate rainbow color once for this frame
354   uint8_t rainbow_r = 0, rainbow_g = 0, rainbow_b = 0;
355   color_filter_calculate_rainbow(time_seconds, &rainbow_r, &rainbow_g, &rainbow_b);
356 
357   // Build the replacement ANSI code string once
358   char rainbow_code[32];
359   snprintf(rainbow_code, sizeof(rainbow_code), "\x1b[38;2;%d;%d;%dm", rainbow_r, rainbow_g, rainbow_b);
360   size_t rainbow_code_len = strlen(rainbow_code);
361 
362   // Check if there are any ANSI codes to replace
363   if (!strstr(ansi_string, "\x1b[38;2;")) {
364     return NULL; // No ANSI codes found, no replacement needed
365   }
366 
367   // Allocate buffer for result (2x size for safety with many replacements)
368   size_t input_len = strlen(ansi_string);
369   char *new_result = SAFE_MALLOC(input_len * 2, char *);
370   if (!new_result) {
371     return NULL;
372   }
373 
374   // Replace all RGB color codes with rainbow color
375   const char *src = ansi_string;
376   char *dst = new_result;
377 
378   while (*src) {
379     const char *ansi_start = strstr(src, "\x1b[38;2;");
380     if (ansi_start) {
381       // Copy everything before this ANSI code
382       size_t before_len = (size_t)(ansi_start - src);
383       memcpy(dst, src, before_len);
384       dst += before_len;
385 
386       // Skip the old ANSI code (find the 'm' that ends it)
387       const char *ansi_end = strchr(ansi_start + 7, 'm');
388       if (ansi_end) {
389         // Copy the rainbow replacement code
390         memcpy(dst, rainbow_code, rainbow_code_len);
391         dst += rainbow_code_len;
392 
393         // Move source past the old code
394         src = ansi_end + 1;
395       } else {
396         // Malformed ANSI code, just copy it
397         *dst++ = *src++;
398       }
399     } else {
400       // No more ANSI codes, copy the rest
401       memcpy(dst, src, strlen(src) + 1);
402       break;
403     }
404   }
405 
406   return new_result;
407 }






References color_filter_calculate_rainbow(), and
    SAFE_MALLOC.


Referenced by mirror_convert_frame(), and
    session_display_convert_to_ascii().






bool rep_is_profitable (uint32_t runlen)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/ascii/output_buffer.h>


Check if run-length encoding is profitable.


Parameters


runlen Run length to check (number of repeated
  characters)


Returns


true if RLE is profitable, false otherwise


Determines whether run-length encoding (RLE) would reduce output
    size compared to emitting individual characters. RLE is profitable when the
    overhead of the repeat sequence is less than the characters it replaces.




RLE PROFITABILITY:


    
  
  • RLE uses format: ESC[runs (e.g., ESC[100X repeats 'X' 100 times)
    • 
  
  • Overhead: ~6-8 bytes for repeat sequence
    • 
  
  • Profit: runlen - overhead bytes saved
    • 
  
  • Profitable when runlen > ~10-15 characters
    • 



Note


Returns true if runlen exceeds RLE overhead threshold.



Threshold is tuned for typical ASCII art character runs.




Definition at line 148 of file output_buffer.c.


148                                         {
149   if (runlen <= 2)
150     return false;
151   uint32_t k = runlen - 1;                           // Extra repetitions beyond the first character
152   uint32_t rep_cost = (uint32_t)(digits_u32(k) + 3); // ESC [ digits b
153   return k > rep_cost;                               // Manual repetition cost vs REP cost
154 }






Referenced by ansi_compress_rle(), image_print(),
    rgb_to_16color_halfblocks_scalar(),
    rgb_to_256color_halfblocks_scalar(),
    rgb_to_halfblocks_scalar(), and
    rgb_to_truecolor_halfblocks_scalar().






char
  * rgb_to_16color_halfblocks_scalar (const uint8_t * rgb, int width, int
  height, int stride_bytes, const char * palette)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/ascii/scalar/halfblock.h>


Convert RGB to 16-color half-blocks using scalar code.


Parameters


rgb RGB pixel data (RGB24 format, 3 bytes per
  pixel)


width Image width in pixels


height Image height in pixels


stride_bytes Row stride in bytes (if 0, calculated as width * 3)


Palette Character palette (unused, for API
  consistency)


pad_height Top padding height (number of newlines to output before
  frame)


Returns


Allocated ASCII string with half-block characters (caller
  must free), or NULL on error


Renders an RGB image using halfblock characters with 16-color ANSI
    codes. Processes 2 rows of source pixels per 1 output line with foreground
    and background color selection from 16-color palette.




Note


Caller must free the returned buffer with free().


Convert RGB to 16-color half-blocks using scalar code.


Definition at line 297 of file
  scalar/halfblock.c.


298                                                             {
299   (void)palette; // Unused for 16-color mode
300   if (width <= 0 || height <= 0)
301     return platform_strdup("");
302   if (stride_bytes <= 0)
303     stride_bytes = width * 3;
304 
305   // Initialize 16-color lookup table
306   ansi_fast_init_16color();
307 
308   outbuf_t ob = {0};
309   // Estimate: per cell ~ 12 bytes (16-color codes); half the rows + newlines + resets
310   size_t est_cells = (size_t)width * ((size_t)(height + 1) / 2);
311   ob.cap = est_cells * 12u + (size_t)((height + 1) / 2) * 8u + 64u;
312   ob.buf = SAFE_MALLOC(ob.cap ? ob.cap : 1, char *);
313   if (!ob.buf)
314     return NULL;
315 
316   // Track 16-color state
317   int cur_fg = -1, cur_bg = -1;
318   static const char HB[3] = {(char)0xE2, (char)0x96, (char)0x80};
319 
320   for (int y = 0; y < height; y += 2) {
321     const uint8_t *rowT = rgb + (size_t)y * (size_t)stride_bytes;
322     const uint8_t *rowB = (y + 1 < height) ? rowT + (size_t)stride_bytes : NULL;
323 
324     int x = 0;
325     while (x < width) {
326       const uint8_t *pT = rowT + (size_t)x * 3u;
327       uint8_t rT = pT[0], gT = pT[1], bT = pT[2];
328       uint8_t color_fg = rgb_to_16color(rT, gT, bT);
329 
330       uint8_t rB = rT, gB = gT, bB = bT;
331       if (rowB) {
332         const uint8_t *pB = rowB + (size_t)x * 3u;
333         rB = pB[0];
334         gB = pB[1];
335         bB = pB[2];
336       }
337       uint8_t color_bg = rgb_to_16color(rB, gB, bB);
338 
339       // Extend run while colors match
340       int j = x + 1;
341       for (; j < width; ++j) {
342         const uint8_t *qT = rowT + (size_t)j * 3u;
343         uint8_t color_fg2 = rgb_to_16color(qT[0], qT[1], qT[2]);
344 
345         uint8_t color_bg2 = color_fg2;
346         if (rowB) {
347           const uint8_t *qB = rowB + (size_t)j * 3u;
348           color_bg2 = rgb_to_16color(qB[0], qB[1], qB[2]);
349         }
350 
351         if (color_fg2 != color_fg || color_bg2 != color_bg)
352           break;
353       }
354       uint32_t run = (uint32_t)(j - x);
355 
356       // Check if transparent
357       bool is_transparent = (rT == 0 && gT == 0 && bT == 0 && rB == 0 && gB == 0 && bB == 0);
358 
359       if (is_transparent) {
360         if (cur_fg != -1 || cur_bg != -1) {
361           emit_reset(&ob);
362           cur_fg = cur_bg = -1;
363         }
364         // Emit spaces for transparent area (no RLE for padding)
365         for (uint32_t k = 0; k < run; ++k) {
366           ob_write(&ob, " ", 1);
367         }
368       } else {
369         // Emit 16-color codes if changed
370         if (cur_fg != color_fg) {
371           char buf[16];
372           char *ptr = append_16color_fg(buf, color_fg);
373           ob_write(&ob, buf, (size_t)(ptr - buf));
374           cur_fg = color_fg;
375         }
376         if (cur_bg != color_bg) {
377           char buf[16];
378           char *ptr = append_16color_bg(buf, color_bg);
379           ob_write(&ob, buf, (size_t)(ptr - buf));
380           cur_bg = color_bg;
381         }
382 
383         ob_write(&ob, HB, 3);
384         if (rep_is_profitable(run)) {
385           emit_rep(&ob, run - 1);
386         } else {
387           for (uint32_t k = 1; k < run; ++k) {
388             ob_write(&ob, HB, 3);
389           }
390         }
391       }
392 
393       x = j;
394     }
395 
396     emit_reset(&ob);
397     if (y + 2 < height) {
398       ob_putc(&ob, '\n');
399     }
400     cur_fg = cur_bg = -1;
401   }
402 
403   ob_term(&ob);
404   return ob.buf;
405 }






References ansi_fast_init_16color(),
    append_16color_bg(), append_16color_fg(),
    outbuf_t::buf, outbuf_t::cap, emit_rep(),
    emit_reset(), ob_putc(), ob_term(), ob_write(),
    platform_strdup(), rep_is_profitable(),
    rgb_to_16color(), and SAFE_MALLOC.

void
  rgb_to_ansi_8bit (int r, int g, int b, int * fg_code, int * bg_code)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/image.h>


Convert RGB to 8-bit ANSI color codes.


Parameters


r Red component (0-255)


g Green component (0-255)


b Blue component (0-255)


fg_code Output foreground color code (must not be NULL, range 0-255)


bg_code Output background color code (must not be NULL, range
  0-255)


Converts RGB color values to 8-bit ANSI color codes (256-color
    palette). Quantizes RGB colors to 256-color palette indices for foreground
    and background colors. Output codes are indices into 256-color ANSI
  palette.




COLOR QUANTIZATION:


    
  
  • RGB colors are quantized to 256-color palette
    • 
  
  • Quantization uses standard 6x6x6 color cube
    • 
  
  • Output codes are palette indices (0-255)
    • 
  
  • Foreground and background codes may differ
    • 



Note


Output parameters are modified to contain palette
  indices.



Color codes are indices into 256-color ANSI palette.




Quantization reduces color accuracy but enables 256-color
  mode.




Definition at line 363 of file foreground.c.


363                                                                        {
364   if (!fg_code || !bg_code) {
365     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "rgb_to_ansi_8bit: fg_code or bg_code is NULL");
366     return;
367   }
368 
369   // Convert RGB to 8-bit color code (216 color cube + 24 grayscale)
370   if (r == g && g == b) {
371     // Grayscale
372     if (r < 8) {
373       *fg_code = 16;
374     } else if (r > 248) {
375       *fg_code = 231;
376     } else {
377       *fg_code = 232 + (r - 8) / 10;
378     }
379   } else {
380     // Color cube: 16 + 36*r + 6*g + b where r,g,b are 0-5
381     int r_level = (r * 5) / 255;
382     int g_level = (g * 5) / 255;
383     int b_level = (b * 5) / 255;
384     *fg_code = 16 + 36 * r_level + 6 * g_level + b_level;
385   }
386   *bg_code = *fg_code; // Same logic for background
387 }






References b, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, and
    SET_ERRNO.






char
  * rgb_to_ansi_bg (int r, int g, int b)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/video/rgba/image.h>


Convert RGB to ANSI background color code.


Parameters


r Red component (0-255)


g Green component (0-255)


b Blue component (0-255)


Returns


Allocated ANSI color code string (caller must free), or
  NULL on error


Converts RGB color values to ANSI background color escape
    sequence. Returns a string containing the ANSI escape sequence for
    background color (e.g., "\033[48;2;255;0;0m" for red
  background).




Note


Returns NULL on error (memory allocation failure).



ANSI string must be freed by caller using free().




ANSI sequence format: ESC[48;2;r;g;bm (truecolor mode).




Color components are clamped to 0-255 range automatically.

360   return color_code;
361 }
